title: Leveraging Llms for Functional Clustering of Microservices
authors: Santiago Di Sabatto, Guillermo Rodriguez, Claudia Marcos, Santiago Vidal
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Microservices architectures often suffer from fragmentation and complexity, making system maintenance, scalability, and evolution increasingly difficult. A key challenge in this context is how to effectively group microservices into coherent functional modules. This paper proposes a novel approach for the functional grouping of microservices using Large Language Models (lLMs) and clustering techniques. Our methodology combines semantic and structural analysis of microservices based on their OpenAPI specifications and was validated using real-world microservice datasets. The results demonstrate that integrating semantic and structural similarities leads to more coherent and manageable groupings. Furthermore, leveraging ChatGPT to generate missing endpoint descriptions significantly improved clustering accuracy, achieving a 60 % higher success rate compared to methods without automatic description generation. Our findings suggest that the proposed approach not only enhances the maintainability and scalability of microservice architectures but also streamlines development and deployment workflows, offering a scalable and efficient solution for service organization.

title: When Video Compression Meets Multimodal Large Language Models: A Unified Paradigm for Cross-Modality Video Compression_supp1-3673193.pdf
authors: Shiqi Wang
abstract: Traditional video compression methods perform well at high bitrates but struggle to preserve fine-grained semantic information at low bitrates. Recently, with the blossoming of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), Cross-modal compression techniques offer prospective solutions for improving video compression under low-bitrate conditions. In this paper, we propose a unified Cross-Modality Video Compression (CMVC) framework that integrates multimodal representations and video generative models. The encoder disentangles video into spatial and temporal components, which are mapped to compact cross modal representations using MLLMs. During decoding, different encoding-decoding modes are employed to acquire various video reconstruction qualities, including Text-Text-to-Video (TT2V) for semantic preservation and Image-Text-to-Video (IT2V) for perceptual consistency. Additionally, we elaborate on an efficient frame interpolation model using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) to improve the perceptual quality. Experimental results demon strate that TT2V achieves effective semantic reconstruction, while IT2V ensures competitive perceptual consistency. These findings suggest the potential of leveraging multimodal priors to improve video compression, offering promising future research directions.

title: A Novel Technique to Discriminate Interference in the Time-Frequency Plane and Localize Mode Crossing
authors: Sylvain Meignen, Vittoria Bruni, Marcelo A. Colominas
abstract: The localization of interference associated with crossing modes in the time-frequency (TF) plane is of significant interest when the objective is to separate these modes. Prior to localizing mode crossings, it is essential to determine whether the observed interference arises from closely spaced modes or from actual mode crossings. In this paper, we propose a novel technique to discriminate between these different types of interference in the TF plane and to subsequently localize mode crossings. The proposed approach exhibits strong adaptability across a wide range of scenarios and demonstrates robustness to noise, as confirmed by simulation results.

title: Sparsity-Constrained NMF Tokenization for Interpretable Medical Signal Classification
authors: Sridharan Sankaran
abstract: The deployment of deep learning in healthcare is often hindered by the “black-box” nature of standard models. Conventional approaches tokenize physiological spectrograms into arbitrary image patches, severing the link between model inputs and physical signal sources. In this letter, we propose a classification approach that uses sparsity-constrained NMF atoms as interpretable features for transparent medical diagnostics. By utilizing Kullback-Leibler divergence NMF, we decompose spectrograms into physically meaningful frequency atoms. Experimental results on the PhysioNet 2016 dataset ($n=3,541$) demonstrate that our method yields diagnostic heatmaps with high Map Sparsity (0.74, median) and Spectral Concentration (0.65), significantly outperforming Grad-CAM baselines ($p < 0.001$). While incurring a statistically significant accuracy trade-off compared to black-box CNNs ($83.6\%$ vs. $90.3\%$, $p < 0.01$), our approach yields structurally interpretable features that align with physiological events, establishing a verifiable foundation for trustworthy medical diagnostics. To facilitate reproducibility, our code is available publicly.

title: A K-/Ka-Band mmWave Radio-Over-Fiber Transceiver Front-End With Co-Designed SiGe EIC and SiPh PIC for Intrasatellite Links
authors: Torben Onselaere, Gaël Jongbloet, Kieran De Bruyn, Marijn Werbrouck, Reinier Broucke, Jasper Jans, Cedric Bruynsteen, Johan Bauwelinck, Nishant Singh, Guy Torfs
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: To meet the demand for low cost, size, weight, and power (C-SWAP) signal routing in the growing satellite and space markets, we propose and demonstrate an all-silicon fully integrated mmWave radio-over-fiber (RoF) transceiver front-end for frequency bands in the range of 18–36 GHz. The opto-electronic front-end consists of a radio frequency integrated circuit (RFIC) with both transmit and receive paths manufactured in a radiation-tolerant SiGe process, connected to the optical components contained on silicon photonic (SiPh) photonic integrated circuits (PICs). The RX uses a Ge PD, followed by a resonant photoreceiver and a resonant driver on the RFIC. The TX uses a resonant predriver and resonant driver on the RFIC, and an SiPh traveling-wave Mach–Zehnder modulator (TW-MZM) on the PIC. The RX and TX on the RFIC exhibit a total DC power consumption of 212 and 230 mW, respectively. The full-RoF-link is characterized through vector network analyzer (VNA) measurements, and its performance is verified through time-domain experiments at different carrier frequencies, resulting in 1-GBd 128-QAM with 5.2% EVM and 5-GBd 64-QAM with 9.1% EVM on a 22-GHz carrier, and 10-GBd 16-QAM/32-QAM with 11.2% EVM on a 28-GHz carrier.

title: Synergistic regulation of polar groups and weak conjugation: preparation of polyimide and improvement of high-temperature energy storage performance
authors: Houyu Wang, Ling Weng, Xiaoming Wang, Xue Li, Lizhu Guan, Lin Zhang, Yingtang Zhang, Li Kong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Polymer film capacitors are widely used in electronic power systems due to their high power density, good self-healing ability, and ease of large-scale production. With the rapid progress of new energy vehicles, aerospace and other fields, there is an urgent need for polymer film capacitors that can work stably under extreme conditions such as high temperature and high electric field. Under the synergistic effect of multiple physical fields such as thermal and electric fields, the electrical conductivity loss of traditional polymer films significantly increases, resulting in a sharp decline in their insulation performance and energy storage characteristics. To overcome this limitation, this study introduces different types and contents of cycloaliphatic structures into polyimide to regulate the π-π conjugation effect within its chain. Then, by controlling the reaction kinetics to retain the polar groups in polyimide, the synergistic effect of cycloaliphatic structure and polar groups is achieved to enhance the energy storage performance of the polymer at high temperatures. Compared with traditional aromatic materials, these materials exhibit significantly improved discharge energy density (U<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">d</sub>) and efficiency (η) under high temperature conditions. It is worth noting that the semi cyclic PI copolymer achieves an excellent energy density of 6.57 J/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> and an efficiency of 88.68 % at 150 ℃; it achieves an excellent energy density of 4.79 J/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> and an efficiency of 82 % at 200 ℃. This enhanced performance is attributed to the polar groups providing controllable polarization ability, increasing dielectric constant, and the cycloaliphatic structure widening the bandgap, suppressing charge transport, and ensuring high breakdown strength. Thus, by finely controlling the molecular chain configuration and aggregation state, the traditional trade-off relationship between dielectric constant and breakdown strength can be broken through, achieving directional design of high-performance insulation materials.

title: Towards Robust Classification of Non-Manual Signals in Korean Sign Language under Extreme Class Imbalance
authors: Seungjae Lee, Jinha Song, Hyeonjoong Cho
abstract: Non-manual signals, such as facial expressions and eye gaze, play a crucial role in sign language communication, comparable to manual signals. However, previous studies in sign language processing have primarily focused on manual signals and often treat non-manual signals only as auxiliary cues for improving recognition or translation performance. Furthermore, most existing sign language datasets lack explicit annotations for non-manual signals due to the high cost of manual labeling, which limits systematic analysis and independent modeling of these signals. This limitation remains an ongoing challenge in sign language research. To address this gap, we conduct a systematic study on non-manual signal classification using the Korean–Korean Sign Language Parallel Corpus 2022 (Version 1.0), one of the few publicly available datasets that provides structured annotations for non-manual signals. However, the dataset suffers from severe class imbalance among annotated non-manual signal labels, making effective learning difficult—particularly for underrepresented classes. To address this problem, we propose an improved framework that systematically analyzes and classifies non-manual signals using long-tail learning techniques, including loss reweighting, resampling, and focal-based optimization. Experimental results show that the proposed approach improves the Macro-F1 score from 0.6065 to 0.7610 compared with the baseline model, achieving substantial gains for minority classes. We further demonstrate that the model can estimate the temporal occurrence of non-manual signals without explicit frame-level annotations, enabling soft-label generation for unlabeled data. These findings provide a practical direction for reducing annotation costs and supporting scalable analysis of non-manual signals in sign language understanding.

title: Towards a Large Language Model for Guarani-Jopara: Challenges, and Early Outcomes
authors: Margarita Ruiz-Olazar, Rubén Díaz, Diego Díaz, Emilio Díaz, Diego Ihara, Marvin Agüero-Torales
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This work presents ongoing research on the development of large language models (LLMs) tailored for GuaraniJopara, a mixed variety of the Guarani language influenced by Spanish and widely spoken in Paraguay. Given the scarcity of digital resources in this language, we constructed a synthetic corpus by translating the Stanford Alpaca dataset into GuaraniJopara using the Google Translate API. The resulting dataset incorporates code-switching phenomena, a core characteristic of Guarani-Jopara. Fine-tuning was performed using HuggingFace's AutoTrain and UnsloothAI's Unsloth on various base models, including Meta LLaMA and Gemma architectures. Model performance was evaluated through both quantitative metrics (SacreBLEU and ROUGE) and qualitative feedback from native speakers. Results indicate that the models capture some linguistic features of Guarani-Jopara, although improvements are needed in naturalness and contextual relevance. This research highlights the feasibility and challenges of applying lLMs to under-resourced, code-switched languages, contributing to the broader goal of inclusive and culturally aware NLP systems.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)

title: 2025 IEEE Women in Engineering (WIE) Forum USA East
authors: N/A

title: A Comprehensive Study on XAI: A User-Centric, Cognitive Workload, and Feasibility-Driven Analysis
authors: Josiah Taylor, Vineetha Menon
abstract: Explainable Artificial Intelligence aims to open up the “black box” of artificial intelligence, thereby improving user trust, decreasing cognitive load, and improving task performance. As the number of machine learning models has grown, so has the number of explanation methods, making it necessary to select the most suitable explanation method for a given context. However, current selection methods often fail to account for all the factors that contribute to an explanation method's suitability. We propose a novel methodology for selecting and evaluating explanation methods. Subjective workload, objective performance, and feasibility are considered. This three-pronged “pitchfork” methodology is put to use by evaluating the performance of LIME and Kernel SHAP in a simulated environment where users are tasked with rescuing people. Quantitative data relating to subjective workload, objective performance, and feasibility is captured and analyzed in order to explore the most suitable explanation for the context.

title: Explainable AI Techniques for Transparent Algorithmic Trading Decisions
authors: Deepak Kumar Giri, Swathi Gangarapu
abstract: The high pace of algorithmic trading development has further enhanced the use of sophisticated machine learning and deep learning concepts, which are able to identify sophisticated patterns in stock time-series information. The existence of these models has the critical drawback of making predictive accuracy and trading performance All these models are big challenges to trust, interpretability, and regulation, even though they offer significant predictive accuracy and trading performance, due to their black-box nature. This study presents a powerful explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) model that aims to enhance the level of transparency in the decision-making of algorithmic trading and ensure high performance. The proposed Hybrid XAI-Model combines the LSTM and the Transformer elements with the post-hoc explainability tools such as SHAP, LIME, and the visualization of attention. The Hybrid XAI-Model has been proven to yield a high level of success compared to the traditional models on the true market datasets and this is based on its ability to give accuracy, precision, recall and profitability variables including annual turnover and the Sharpe ratio. In addition, the stress-test analysis indicates that the stability of the explanation in volatile and extreme market environments is good which show the resilience of the model and its reliability. Evaluations at inference time have ensured that the model could be used to satisfy real-time latency performance. The findings highlight the value of explainability as one of the fundamental aspects of the next generation AI-based financial systems, as it increases clarity of an individual model, promotes greater risk management and regulatory coherence. The research makes a significant contribution of providing practitioners, researchers and policymakers with a scalable, interpretable and high-performing architecture of transparent algorithmic trading.

title: Residual-Constrained and Future-Aware Correlation Filter for UAV Object Tracking
authors: Bing He, Hui Zhou, FangKai Zhao, XinYue Wang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: In this paper, we propose a correlation filter for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) object tracking based on response residual constraints and environmental perception. First, the single exponential smoothing prediction algorithm is adopted for context-aware regularization, enabling the model to suppress potential future interference. Second, a response-weighted background residual learning mechanism is introduced to achieve differentiated learning of background regions and enhance the discriminative ability of the tracker. Furthermore, the ADMM is employed to optimize the model, thereby obtaining more reliable filter coefficients. Extensive experiments are conducted on three challenging public UAV tracking benchmarks, which verify the effectiveness and robustness of our tracker. Meanwhile, it achieves real-time tracking speed (54 frames/s) on a single CPU.

title: Research on Steel Surface Defect Detection Algorithm Based on Improved YOLOv11
authors: Xinyi Guo, Hongmin Ren
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: To address the challenges of missed detection caused by significant scale variations, irregular shapes of steel surface defects, and complex background interference in industrial production, this paper proposes an enhanced detection algorithm named YOLO-ODM based on YOLOv11. The approach incorporates a fully dimensional dynamic convolution module named ODC3k2 to strengthen the dynamic feature extraction capability for multi-scale steel defects; introduces the EMA attention mechanism into the backbone network to enhance the model's perception of defects and effectively suppress background noise; and adopts the WIoUv3 loss function to improve robustness and localization accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves mAP50, mAP50:95, and recall rates of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$79.2 \%, 45.8 \%$</tex>, and 78.5%, respectively, confirming its effectiveness in steel defect detection.

title: Cascaded H-Bridge-Based Battery Storage Systems: A Distributed Control Approach for SoC Equalization of Second-Life Batteries
authors: Carlos Smart, Andrés Mora, Claudio Burgos Mellado
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: The objective of this paper is to validate a novel consensus-based distributed control scheme for a cascaded Hbridge (CHB) battery energy storage system integrating heterogeneous second-life batteries (SLBs). The inherent variability in capacity of SLBs is the critical challenge this work addresses, justifying the need for a specific control approach. The proposed modular architecture groups submodules (SMs) into clusters based on shared degradation and residual capacity characteristics. The distributed control combines a central controller (CC) for inter-cluster SoC balancing and grid power management, with local controllers (LCs) that employ a consensus-based algorithm to equalize the SoC within each cluster. This dual-level strategy is essential to mitigate the individual parameter mismatch of the SLBs. Detailed simulation studies demonstrate effective and coordinated SoC balancing at both inter-cluster and intra-cluster levels, confirming that the distributed control ensures stable performance, making the CHB-based SLB system a promising, cost-effective solution for grid support.

title: Performance Evaluation of Deep Learning Architectures for Flood Forecasting: Optimizing Sequential Pattern Recognition in Century-Scale Rainfall Series
authors: Ayushi Chakraborty, Vivekananda Mukherjee, Manabendra Maiti
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Analysis for Smart Systems (SIGNASS)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative evaluation of machine learning and deep learning models for flood prediction in West Bengal using 124 years of historical rainfall data (1901-2024). Lag variables and moving averages were added to the dataset in order to have equal flood and non-flood classes. KNearest Neighbors had the highest accuracy (91.7%), Support Vector Machine had the best discrimination (ROC-AUC = 0.979), and the random forest was the most robust and explainable (accuracy =87.5%, ROC-AUC =0.958, balanced F1 =0.88). The value of winter rainfall as a predictor of summer was shown by Explainable AI (SHAP), and uncertainty was measured through consensus scoring and confidence intervals, which increased reliability. The future projections (2023-2035) portray three high-risk years (2028, 2031, 2033), with 2033 having unanimity between the models that severe floods are going to occur. The study provides a framework that combines feature engineering, interpretability, and uncertainty quantification to enhance the flood early warning system at the regional level and with data.

title: Modeling Income Declaration Behavior in Chilean Student Loans: A Dataset and Baseline Predictive Analysis of the FSCU Program
authors: Álex Paz, Broderick Crawford, Eric Monfroy, Andrés Yáñez, Ricardo Soto, José Barrera-García, Felipe Cisternas-Caneo, Benjamín López Cortés
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This work presents a novel dataset designed to support predictive modeling of income declaration behavior in the University Credit Solidarity Fund (FSCU) program of Chile. Using institutional records from the Pontificia Universidad Católica de Valparaíso, we prepared a structured and anonymized dataset that captures academic, financial, and demographic variables relevant to student loan administration. We describe the data extraction and preprocessing pipeline, including entity modeling, feature engineering, and the creation of a relational database. As a baseline evaluation, we conducted initial experiments using 15 machine learning algorithms via PyCaret, aiming to predict whether a student who previously submitted income declarations would cease to do so. Although no hyperparameter tuning or data balancing was applied, the results offer a solid foundation for future experimentation. Boosting-based classifiers-particularly XGBoost-achieved the best performance, with accuracy and AUC exceeding 96 %. This contribution addresses the lack of publicly available, context-sensitive datasets for modeling conditional repayment schemes in higher education finance. Future work includes dataset publication, class balancing, and explainability analysis.

title: Adaptive Codebook Design and Beam Training for RIS-Aided Communication Systems With Hardware Constraints
authors: Jiahao Gao, Shuhao Zeng, Boya Di, Lin Chang, Lianlin Li, Wei Xiang Jiang, Lingyang Song
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) has emerged as a promising solution to enable ultra-massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) for 6G wireless communications. To direct the transmitted beam towards users with low pilot overhead, multi-resolution codebook-based beam training has been proposed for RIS-aided communication systems, which employs codewords with varying beamwidths to progressively narrow the search range and identify the optimal beam direction. However, the hardware constraints of the RIS such as the limited control capability, mutual coupling and manufacturing tolerances have not been fully considered, which may result in non-ideal beam patterns and misleading beam search directions, thereby degrading the overall beam training performance. To address this issue, in this paper, we revisit the multi-resolution codebook design and beam training scheme by taking the RIS hardware constraints into account. First, we propose a wide-beam generation method via multi-beam superposition, where the RIS reflection coefficients are optimized to achieve high beam gain with suppressed undesired fluctuations. Then, to mitigate the impact of the residual fluctuations in beam patterns, a posterior-based beam training scheme is proposed to adaptively correct erroneous beam search directions, thereby improving the beam training accuracy. To experimentally evaluate the proposed scheme, we implement a prototype RIS with <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$32\times 32$ </tex-math></inline-formula> reflective elements and deploy a 26 GHz millimeter-wave RIS-aided wireless communication testbed. Numerical and experimental results indicate that our approach achieves low training overhead and a data rate close to the exhaustive search method.

title: Biosystems Engineering for the Digital Transformation of Agriculture: Integrating Hardware, Software, and Global Collaboration
authors: Fernando Fuentes-Peñailillo, Ma. Luisa del Campo-Hitschfeld, Emmanuel Torres-Quezada, Gilda Carrasco Silva, Gaston Lefranc
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Biosystems Engineering has evolved into a multidisciplinary field that integrates engineering principles, life sciences, and data-driven technologies to address critical challenges related to sustainability, climate adaptation, and resource efficiency in agricultural and environmental systems. This article presents a comprehensive framework for agricultural digitalization that integrates low-cost sensing hardware, intelligent software platforms, and coordinated international collaboration. The study synthesizes experiences and developments led by academic and applied research teams from Chile, the United States, and the Dominican Republic, illustrating how Internet of Things (IoT) networks, cloud-based data infrastructures, and decision-support systems can be combined to monitor, automate, and optimize biological and environmental processes across diverse production contexts. By integrating system-level engineering design, data analytics, and collaborative governance models, Biosystems Engineering is positioned as a key enabler of scalable, adaptable solutions for sustainable agricultural and environmental management across Chile and Latin America.

title: A Machine Learning-Based Multi-Dimensional Text Complexity Model for English Writing Assessment
authors: Ling Qin
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Automated essay scoring (AES) has been widely applied in intelligent foreign language education. However, many existing AES methods remain difficult to interpret, rely on a limited set of linguistic indicators, or provide insufficient theoretical motivation for feature design. To address these issues, this study proposes a feature-based AES framework that operationalizes multi-dimensional text complexity through fine-grained, theory-aligned indicators spanning writing accuracy, lexical sophistication and diversity, morphological and syntactic complexity, and discourse cohesion/coherence. Using the ASAP dataset (Prompt 1, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=1,783$</tex>), we extract 327 interpretable complexity features with established NLP tools and train a machine learning scoring model under a transparent preprocessing and validation protocol. In addition to the primary linear model, we clarify the compared machine learning algorithms and the baseline selection strategy, and we incorporate an interpretability analysis that visualizes the top influential indicators and their positive/negative directions. Results show that the proposed model achieves robust agreement with human ratings (QWK =0.843 on the validation set) and yields pedagogically meaningful feature contributions. The findings suggest that multi-dimensional complexity indicators provide a coherent and interpretable basis for AES feature design and can offer theory-informed insights into second language writing ability.

title: Energy Management Through Dynamic Tariff Schemes for the Green Energy and Battery Storage Systems
authors: Sandali Hettikanda, Muhammad Waseem, M. Asim Amin
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on Power Science and Engineering (ICPSE)
abstract: This research investigates the integration of Photovoltaic (PV) generation in a Building Energy Management System (BEMS) to improve efficiency, reduce grid dependency, and enhance economic benefits. The study is structured around two case studies. The first explores battery energy storage management, applying Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP) to optimize charging and discharging for cost efficiency and supply-demand balance. The second examines energy trading, comparing dynamic and static tariff mechanisms to demonstrate how dynamic pricing can increase export power and maximize earnings. The main objective of these case studies is to achieve sustainable, efficient, and economically viable energy systems. While demonstrating the revolutionary impact of BEMS in future energy systems, optimized battery storage facilitates flexibility and resilience to demand fluctuations. In addition, dynamic tariff models create new possibilities for economic rewards and stimulate active participation in the energy market. These key findings show that BEMS improve efficiency while enabling income generation to increase through exporting and sustainability.

title: SML-Net: A Novel Deep Learning Network for the Prediction of the SML Index
authors: Mohamed Abouhashem Salama, Purbi Adhya, Edmund Spencer, Mohamed Shaban
abstract: Magnetic substorms which are caused by the interaction of the earth magnetospheric and ionospheric layers with the solar wind may negatively impact the satellite communication systems. Several research studies have used the SuperMAG Lower Index (i.e., SML-Index) to detect the onset of the magnetic substorms. It is also worth mentioning that the use of machine and deep learning for the prediction of the SML-Index has not yet been explored in the literature. In this paper, we proposed a novel deep learning framework named the SML-Net that was able to predict future ten-minute time-frames of the SML-Index based on the trend, seasonality and residual components of past sixty-minute time-frames of the same index at a relatively low normalized root mean squared error of 0.34 and comparatively high coefficient of determination of 92.9% with respect to four benchmark models including the stacked long short term memory (LSTM), the stacked gated recurrent unit (GRU), the temporal convolutional network (TCN) and the transformer encoder.

title: A Hybrid VGG-ResNet Architecture for Fine-Grained Image Classification Enhancement
authors: Bei Xie, Kairu Peng
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Fine-grained image classification has become a research challenge in computer vision due to challenges such as subtle inter-class differences and significant intra-class differences. In order to address the problem of the limited feature expression ability of traditional single-network models, this paper proposes a dual-path heterogeneous fusion architecture (DPHF) based on VGG and ResNet. The model preserves the high-frequency detail perception ability of the VGG network and the deep semantic representation advantage of ResNet through parallel feature extraction paths, and designs a dynamic feature fusion module to realize the complementary enhancement of multi-source features. Experiments on the Stanford Dog and CUB-200-2011 show that the proposed DPHF (Concat) algorithm significantly outperforms the single network benchmark, and the effectiveness of the heterogeneous feature fusion strategy is verified through ablation experiments, demonstrating the superiority of the dual-path architecture in capturing fine-grained differences.

title: MHDMC-Net: A Lightweight Hybrid Architecture with Multi-Head Dynamic Convolution and Adaptive Gating for Accurate Brain Tumor Classification
authors: Xiaoxia Qi, Yichi Huang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Brain tumor classification from magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) remains a critical yet challenging task in medical diagnosis due to subtle inter-class variations and limited annotated datasets. In this paper, we propose a novel lightweight hybrid architecture that synergistically combines Multi-Head Dynamic Multi-scale Convolutional (MHDMC) modules with Adaptive Multi-scale Gated Linear Units (AM-GLU) for accurate and efficient tumor classification. The MHDMC module employs dynamic kernel selection to adaptively capture multi-scale spatial features across different receptive fields, while the AM-GLU enhances channel-wise discriminability through learnable gating mechanisms. Extensive experiments on a publicly available brain tumor MRI dataset demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance with 99.39% accuracy, 99.42% precision, and only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 3 M}$</tex> parameters-a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 7. 3 \%}$</tex> reduction compared to ViTBase (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 6. 6 M}$</tex> parameters) and 91.9% reduction vs. Swin-T (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 8. 3 M}$</tex> parameters) under identical training conditions (50 epochs, AdamW optimizer, same augmentation pipeline), achieving 3.55% and 2.44% higher accuracy respectively while delivering superior diagnostic precision. The proposed framework offers a compelling solution for resource-constrained clinical deployment scenarios.

title: Photovoltaic Power Forecasting and Mechanism Analysis Based on Interpretable Deep Learning
authors: Taizhen Wang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Accurate short-term photovoltaic (PV) power forecasting is crucial for reliable power system operation under high renewable energy penetration. However, PV generation exhibits strong intermittency, nonlinear dynamics, and long-range temporal dependencies, which challenge conventional forecasting approaches. To address these issues, this paper proposes an Informer-based framework for short-term PV power forecasting that enables efficient modeling of long temporal sequences with reduced computational complexity. The proposed model employs probabilistic sparse self-attention to capture global temporal dependencies while maintaining scalability. Extensive experiments are conducted on real-world multi-station PV datasets, and the proposed approach is compared with statistical models, classical machine learning methods, and deep learning baselines. The results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves consistently superior performance across all stations, with an average RMSE of 3.47 MW, MAE of 1.98 MW, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> of 0.95. Furthermore, SHAP-based interpretability analysis is incorporated to quantify feature contributions and reveal nonlinear and thresholddependent effects, enhancing the transparency of the forecasting process. The proposed framework provides an accurate, efficient, and interpretable solution for short-term photovoltaic power forecasting.

title: Battery Degradation and Reuse WITH Quantum Optimization: A Data-Driven Approach for the Circular Economy
authors: Sebastian Moya, Ismael Soto, Sebastian Seguel, Enrique Espina, Cesar Azurdia
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This study investigates the capacity degradation variability among lithium-ion battery cells of the same type, highlighting the significant influence of operational conditions, environmental factors, and manufacturing differences on battery lifespan. A technical end-of-life (EOL) threshold (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$C_{\text {EOL }}$</tex>) is established to uniformly identify batteries suitable for secondlife applications, such as stationary storage and low-power electronics. The implementation of a K-Means clustering algorithm effectively classifies retired batteries into distinct groups based on their degradation state, enabling optimized reuse strategies tailored to each cluster's characteristics. Additionally, a comparative analysis of classical and quantum computing complexities demonstrates the promising potential of quantum algorithms, particularly QAOA, to substantially reduce computational costs in large-scale battery data processing and optimization tasks. The findings support the adoption of quantitative and automated methodologies that enhance circular economy practices by minimizing premature battery disposal and maximizing resource utilization within the evolving energy landscape.

title: MFND: A Multimodal Fusion Framework for Noise-Robust Fault Detection in Microservice Architectures
authors: Haokun Fan, Cheng Zhang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Microservices architecture has become the main paradigm of cloud computing because it is easy to scale and agility. However, its distributed property brings some problems to fault detection, such as complex service dependency and various monitoring data (logs, metrics, Traces). The existing methods often fail to cope with the noise of the raw data effectively, are unable to make full use of the cross-modal correlation of data, resulting in inaccurate fault diagnosis or misjudgment. To solve this problem, this paper puts forward MFND a new multimodal fault detection framework based on dynamic graph construction and robust feature learning of logs, metrics and traces. Framework describes service dependencies in the form of dynamic graphs and retains important features by filtering out noises, and uses Hybrid GNN for adaptively capturing the system state transition. Experiments on public data sets show that MFND can achieve the leading detection effect in the industry and provide the optimal deployment configuration through parameter analysis, and it is proved to be an effective and stable fault detection solution for dynamic microservice environment.

title: TAQ: Task-Aligned Mixed-Precision Quantization for Object Detection
authors: Ningfu Yang, Hairui Guo, Xiaoyi Yang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Deploying real-time object detectors on resource-constrained edge devices is a critical challenge. While mixed-precision quantization offers a solution, conventional metrics relying on global reconstruction may waste bit-width on background rather than targets. To address this, we propose TAQ, a mixed-precision quantization framework for object detection. TAQ integrates a Task-Aligned Sensitivity (TAS) metric with a target-aware RoI mask. By excluding background regions during sensitivity evaluation, TAS ensures that quantization decisions are driven solely by target fidelity. Furthermore, a quantization parameter decoupling mechanism is proposed to separate dynamic range estimation from precision control, enabling efficient search without repetitive training. Experiments on the COCO dataset show that TAQ achieves a superior accuracy-efficiency trade-off compared to state-of-the-art methods.

title: Predictive Maintenance in Textile Manufacturing Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Nelson Chambi, Celso Sanga, Alejandra Sanga, Piero Sanga
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This article addresses predictive maintenance (PdM) applied to textile machinery using artificial intelligence, a key approach to improving efficiency and reducing operating costs. The objective is to optimize equipment lifespan through early fault detection, avoiding costly repairs and unplanned downtime. The challenge lies in intensive operating conditions and high production demand, which make failure prediction difficult with traditional methods. The methodology includes continuous monitoring of critical parameters (vibrations, temperature, humidity, yarn tension, and component wear) using sensors and real-time data analysis. These data are processed with artificial intelligence and machine learning techniques to identify patterns that precede failures. The results demonstrate that PdM can reduce maintenance costs by 8% and increase equipment availability by 10 %, achieving greater productivity and quality in textile manufacturing.

title: Assessment of Driver Cognitive Load in Autonomous Driving Using Extended Reality Simulation
authors: Seth Hatchett, Connor Viana, Arman Sargolzaei, Saman Sargolzaei
abstract: In this study, an extended reality-based driving simulation combined with electroencephalography (EEG), heart rate variability (HRV), and eye tracking data was used to assess the impact of advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) on cognitive load, which is the proportion of a driver's total cognitive resources needed to complete the driving task. The EEG data, processed using both multitaper spectral estimation (MT) and wavelet transform (WT), showed that AI-assisted steering and fully autonomous driving reduced cognitive load, whereas AIassisted throttle and manual driving did not significantly affect cognitive load relative to the baseline. This was indicated by decreased alpha, theta, and beta for fully autonomous driving and AI-assisted steering trials. HRV data further supported this trend, with the root mean square of successive inter-beat interval differences (RMSSD) increasing during manual and AI-assisted steering trials, indicating reduced cognitive load during these conditions. Eye tracking data also supported the EEG trend, with an increased total absolute change in eye motion speed variance during AI-assisted throttle trials. This exploratory study provides preliminary evidence that different ADAS functions may differentially influence driver cognitive load and engagement.

title: A Software-Defined Testbed for Quantifying Deauthentication Resilience in Modern Wi-Fi Networks
authors: Alex Carbajal, Asma Jodeiri Akbarfam
abstract: Wi-Fi deauthentication attacks remain a practical denial-of-service (DoS) threat by exploiting unprotected management frames to disrupt client connectivity. In this work, we introduce a software-defined testbed to measure Wi-Fi resilience to deauthentication attacks. We experimentally evaluate five wireless security configurations: open networks, WPA1, WPA2 without Protected Management Frames (PMF), WPA2 with PMF, and WPA3. Using controlled experiments, we measure client disconnection rates, packet injection volume, and time-to-disruption under each configuration. Packet-level behavior is analyzed using standard wireless auditing tools. Open networks, WPA1, and WPA2 without PMF proved entirely vulnerable to deauthentication, while no successful attacks were observed for WPA2 with PMF or WPA3 under tested conditions. These findings confirm the effectiveness of management-frame protection and highlight the continued risk posed by legacy or misconfigured wireless deployments.

title: Availability Attacks without an Adversary: Evidence from Enterprise LANs
authors: Rajendra Paudyal, Rajendra Upadhyay, Al Nahian Bin Emran, Lisa Donnan, Duminda Wijesekera
abstract: Denial-of-Service (DoS) conditions in enterprise networks are commonly attributed to malicious actors. However, availability can also be compromised by benign non-malicious insider behavior. This paper presents an empirical study of a production enterprise LAN that demonstrates how routine docking and undocking of user endpoints repeatedly trigger rapid recalculations of the control plane of the Rapid Spanning Tree Protocol (RSTP) [1]. Although protocol-compliant and nonmalicious, these events introduce transient forwarding disruptions of approximately 2-4 seconds duration that degrade real-time streaming (voice and video) services while remaining largely undetected by conventional security monitoring. We map this phenomenon to the NIST and MITRE insider threat frameworks, characterizing it as an unintentional insider-driven availability breach, and demonstrate that explicit edge-port configuration effectively mitigates the condition without compromising loop prevention.

title: Investigation into Mathematical Modeling of Collision Avoidance Zones Under the Detection and Avoidance Framework
authors: Yuan Zhang, Jialiang Yuan
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) technology is experiencing rapid and ever-advancing development, and the potential safety hazards such as collisions and conflicts arising therefrom have become increasingly prominent. Building upon the framework of the DAIDALUS algorithm, this study develops a mathematical model oriented towards horizontal trajectory conflict prevention. The model first employs the Cauchy inequality, in conjunction with the dynamic characteristics of UAVs' noninstantaneous maneuvers, to establish a mathematical description of trajectory conflicts. Experimental validation demonstrates that the proposed mathematical model exhibits higher accuracy in the computation of collision avoidance intervals and enables reliable collision avoidance functionality.

title: Rainfall Observation - A Comparison of Satellite and Ground-Based Remote Sensing
authors: Soumyasree Guin, Sumon Kumar Mondal, Sohom Roy Chowdhury, Swastika Chakraborty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Analysis for Smart Systems (SIGNASS)
abstract: Short-term spatiotemporal variability of rainfall over Agartala, a Northeast hill station, has been observed. The study provides an intercomparison of surface rain gauge measurements, ground-based Doppler Weather Radar (DWR) observations at two heights (35 m and 7517 m) and satelliteobserved precipitation on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$22{}^{\text{nd}}$</tex> August, 2022. Near-surface radar <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(35 \mathrm{m})$</tex> measurement is affected by terrain-induced clutter and reflectivity and high-altitude radar (7517 m) measurement consistently suffers due to detection error. GPM captures most of the rainfall event with underestimation. Performance analysis indicates that near-surface radar (35 m) shows higher correlation with the gauge (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P C C} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{0. 5 5 2}$</tex>), while GPM shows the lower RMSE (2.54). Overall, the study highlights the strengths and limitations of each remote observation system, highlights their regional coverage. Satellite and radar products can complement ground-based observations in hilly terrain with suitable adjustment.

title: Aquaculture Net Cage Breakage Detection Based on improved YOLOv8
authors: Jincheng Wan, Lei Li
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: To address the challenges of insufficient accuracy and real-time performance in detecting defects within aquaculture cage structures, this study proposes an enhanced YOLOv8n-based detection framework through three structural innovations. Firstly, we reconstruct the C2f module by substituting standard Bottleneck layers with Partial-Block units, forming a C2f-Partial architecture that preserves network integrity while strengthening multi-scale feature representation through hierarchical residual connections. Secondly, a Share-Conv Detection Head (SCDH) incorporating Group Normalization Convolution (GN-Conv) is designed to streamline feature aggregation, achieving 39.5% reduction in computational load (3.2 GFLOPs) and 42% model compression (2.45 MB). Thirdly, a novel Focal-SIoU loss function synergistically integrates Focal penalty weights with SIoU geometric constraints, effectively balancing critical sample prioritization and bounding box regression accuracy. Experimental validation demonstrates significant performance gains: precision (92.1%), recall (89.8%), and mAP (91.1%) show absolute increments of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.3 \%, 4.5 \%$</tex>, and 3.5% respectively over baseline YOLOv8n. The optimized model maintains real-time inference capability while outperforming mainstream detectors in speed-accuracy trade-offs, as evidenced by its reduced parameter footprint and computational intensity. These advancements establish a computationally efficient solution for underwater mesh defect identification, particularly suited for edge deployment in resource-constrained aquaculture monitoring systems.

title: Automated Evaluation of Authentication Features of Web Pages from the Low Vision User Perspective
authors: Zeyu He, Suzanna Schmeelk, Jose Florez-Arango, Helen Petrie
abstract: Ensuring secure and accessible authentication is crucial for protecting user data while maintaining inclusivity for individuals with disabilities. However, security mechanisms such as CAPTCHAs, password strength meters, and complex password policies often create barriers that hinder accessibility. This study conducted an automated evaluation of authentication features across 39 widely used websites, assessing CAPTCHA implementation, password strength indicators, and policy transparency using Selenium-based automation. The findings revealed that only 7.7% of analyzed websites implemented CAPTCHA, yet none provided an audio alternative, limiting accessibility for visually impaired users. Password-based authentication remained the dominant mechanism, with 71.8% of websites including password fields; however, only 28.2% utilized password strength meters, and among them, just 23.1% incorporated ARIA attributes necessary for screen reader compatibility. Additionally, password policies were frequently hidden, with only 12.8% of sites displaying complexity requirements before user input. The presence of anti-bot measures on certain websites further restricted automated accessibility evaluations, suggesting that authentication mechanisms dynamically adapt based on user interactions. These findings underscore the need for inclusive security design, ensuring that CAPTCHA alternatives, screen reader-compatible password meters, and transparent password policies are integrated into authentication systems. Future work should explore biometric authentication and adaptive multifactor authentication as potential solutions to enhance both security and accessibility. Expanding automated testing frameworks could also improve large-scale evaluations of authentication barriers, contributing to more inclusive and secure digital environments.

title: EduCore: A Privacy-Aware LLM-Driven Education Framework with Intelligent Multi-Agent Support
authors: Debashis Das, Tenicka Norwood, Pushpita Chatterjee, Uttam Ghosh
abstract: Educational institutions still depend on manual and disconnected systems for administration, teaching, assessment, and student support. This increases faculty workload, slows academic processes, and limits personalized learning. Most existing digital platforms only store content and do not actively assist teachers or students. Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) make it possible to build intelligent systems that can support and automate routine academic tasks. Therefore, this paper presents the design and implementation of a privacy-preserving, LLM-driven agentic education framework that integrates smart administration, AI-assisted teaching, personalized learning, and workforce-oriented academic advising into a unified system. The framework uses multiple AI agents, each designed for a specific task, and runs on locally deployed LLMs. A retrieval-based approach is used to ensure responses are accurate, relevant, and secure. By keeping data within the institution, the system protects privacy and reduces operational costs. The proposed system also reduces faculty workload, improves efficiency in academic processes, increases student engagement, and provides better personalized support. Overall, this work shows that agent-based AI can modernize education systems practically and securely to support both educators and students without replacing human decision-making.

title: Enhanced MIT Coffee Can Radar with Integrated Infrared Imaging for Target Detection and Classification for Experiential Learning Platform
authors: Isaac Anokye, Hidalgo Mudonhi, Goodwill Kwenda, Delores Baker
abstract: This paper presents an enhanced version of the MIT Coffee Can Radar platform that integrates a softwaredefined radio (SDR) and an infrared (IR) imaging sensor to create a low-cost, modular learning system for radar and signal processing education. The proposed system replaces traditional analog radar components with an ADALM-Pluto SDR and an IR camera, enabling basic sensor fusion for improved target detection. Preliminary experimental results demonstrate range and Doppler detection capabilities, as well as qualitative improvements in target confirmation using thermal imagery. Designed as a reusable educational module, the platform bridges theoretical instruction and hands-on experimentation, providing an accessible framework for undergraduate engineering education.

title: Engineering Management for AI-Driven Software Quality Assurance: Practices, Challenges, and Future Direction
authors: Srinivas Reddy Kosna, Subba Rao Katragadda
abstract: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into software systems has introduced unprecedented complexities and opportunities for quality assurance. This paper explores the evolving landscape of software quality engineering management in the age of AI. It examines the critical challenges and best practices for managing the quality of AI-driven software, from data acquisition to model deployment and maintenance. We present a comprehensive framework that synthesizes established software engineering principles with emerging machine learning (ML) development lifecycles. The paper analyzes key engineering management practices, including automated test generation, defect prediction, and continuous model validation, drawing on case studies and industry research. Furthermore, it discusses the future of AI in quality assurance, highlighting the potential of Large Language Models (LLMs) and knowledge graphs to enable more autonomous and intelligent testing paradigms. This paper aims to provide a valuable resource for engineering managers, QA professionals, and researchers navigating the transition to AI-centric software development.

title: Research on Post-Processing Methods for Object Detection in Electrical Secondary Drawings for Substation Engineering Applications
authors: Xiaoxu Wang, Houyuan Li, Congying Wu, Hongming Shen, Peili Yan
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of digitalization and intelligence in power systems, the automatic recognition of electrical drawings plays a crucial role in engineering design, construction, operation, and maintenance. In recent years, deep learning-based object detection methods have achieved high detection accuracy in electrical drawing recognition tasks. However, their output results often fail to directly meet the stringent requirements for high reliability and safety in engineering applications. Even with high accuracy in offline testing, a small number of recognition errors can pose significant risks in practical engineering scenarios. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a post-processing method for object detection in electrical secondary drawing recognition for substation engineering applications. By introducing confidence interval grading and a conformal prediction mechanism, effective risk control of detection results is achieved. First, based on the confidence and category uncertainty output by the object detection model, a data-driven approach is adopted to classify detected objects into different confidence levels, thereby distinguishing highly reliable results from potentially high-risk ones. Second, the conformal prediction method is applied specifically to mediumand low-confidence detected objects to generate prediction sets with statistical coverage guarantees, facilitating manual rapid calibration and reducing the cost of manual review. In the end-to-end engineering closed-loop evaluation, the proposed method achieves engineering-level recognition accuracy while maintaining a high degree of automation.

title: Development of a Gomoku System Based on 2D Array Modeling and Algorithm Optimization
authors: Wenmin Rui, Shengsheng Liu
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Taking the engineering development of digital games as the core, focusing on key technologies such as two-dimensional array modeling, four-direction scanning optimization algorithm and modular programming, the efficient development path of Gobang system is constructed. Based on Python 3.9 + and Pygame 2.1 + environment, this paper designs a four-stage engineering mode of ' demand analysis-algorithm design-modular implementation-system test ', completes the digital modeling of 15 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\times 15$</tex> chessboard, clarifies the design of draw decision logic and boundary fault tolerance mechanism, reduces the time complexity of winning algorithm from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O} 15 \times 15 \times 4 \times 15$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O} 7 \times 7 \times 4 \times 5$</tex>, and realizes the development of full-function system. Through 80 sets of development sample control tests (the experimental group adopts the optimized path, and the control group is traditional linear development), the results show that the code reuse rate of the experimental group is increased by 37.2 %, the algorithm execution efficiency is increased by 41.5 %, the functional integrity compliance rate is increased by 28.6 %, the module coupling degree is reduced by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$65.5 \%(\mathrm{p}<0.001)$</tex>, and the abnormal error rate is reduced from 12.3 % to 3.1 %. The technical solution is suitable for the development of small and medium-sized digital games, and provides a reusable paradigm for the landing of core computer technology in interactive applications.

title: A Power Load Forecasting Uncertainty Quantification Method Combining CNN-BiLSTM and Gaussian Process Regression
authors: Yuhao Zhang, Xin Dong
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Power load forecasting is a core link in the safe operation and optimal dispatching of power systems. Traditional point forecasting methods fail to quantify the potential uncertainty, which brings risks to the decision-making of power systems. To solve this problem, a power load forecasting uncertainty quantification method combining Convolutional Neural Network-Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (CNN-BiLSTM) and Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) is proposed. First, the CNN-BiLSTM model is constructed to extract local spatial features and bidirectional temporal dependencies of multi-dimensional load influencing factors, and obtain high-precision point forecasting results. Then, the prediction error of the CNN-BiLSTM model is taken as the input of the GPR model, and the GPR is used to model the probability distribution of the error, so as to realize the uncertainty quantification of the load forecasting. Finally, experiments are carried out on the actual power load dataset. The results show that compared with the traditional CNN-LSTM, BiLSTM and GPR single models, the proposed method not only has higher point forecasting accuracy (the mean absolute percentage error is reduced by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15.3 \%-28.6 \%$</tex>), but also can effectively quantify the forecasting uncertainty. The prediction interval coverage probability (PICP) reaches more than 93% under the 95% confidence level, and the prediction interval normalized average width (PINAW) is kept at a low level, which provides a reliable decision-making basis for the safe and economic operation of the power system.

title: Vision Transformer-Based Explainable Framework for Automated Lung Cancer Identification
authors: Gopal Dutta, Deepta Roy, Kaushik Naskar, Neepan Biswas, Md Ashifuddin Mondal, Subhram Das, Papri Ghosh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Analysis for Smart Systems (SIGNASS)
abstract: Globally, lung cancer is one of the most responsible reason for the highest number of cancer-related deaths, causing an estimated 1.8 million deaths each year. Accurate and Timely precise identification of lung cancer subtypes is highly crucial for the appropriate treatment planning of patients. This paper presents a deep learning architecture based on the “Vision Transformer” (ViT) for the automated classification of lung cancer histopathological images which can be divided into three categories: benign lung tissue, lung adenocarcinoma, and lung squamous cell carcinoma. The proposed system leverages patchbased embeddings and multihead self-attention mechanisms to capture global contextual relationships and learn discriminative morphological representations. The challenge of data scarcity is alleviated by transfer learning from ImageNet-21k pre-trained weights, while end-to-end regulation and fine-tuning is performed on the LC25000 dataset that consists of 15,000 histopathology images. The performance of the Vision Transformer model in our simulation model proved to be exceptional, achieving 0.99% accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, surpassing all NN-based baselines. Thats the key reason for using this model in order to get better results which will further enhance the accuracy of the required classification.

title: Enhancing Cross-Modal Person Re-Identification through Fine-Grained Feature Fusion
authors: Boning Zhang, Peishun Liu, Hengtao Wang, Mengqi Luo, Quanjie Dou, Yibao Song
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Person re-identification, abbreviated as Re-ID, focuses on identifying and matching individual pedestrians in diverse application scenarios. Traditional single-modal methods often struggle with poor image quality, severe occlusion, or high appearance similarity. Aiming to resolve these dilemmas, crossmodal Re-ID based on text-image fusion has gained increasing attention. Within the scope of this paper, we propose FGCM-Net, a fine-grained cross-modal Re-ID architecture that delves into the correlative relationships existing between textual and visual feature representations. FGCM-Net integrates a Text-Driven Multi-Modal Feature Fusion Module (TDMF) to enhance crossmodal interaction and a Fine-Grained Relationship Matching Module (FRM) to enable adaptive feature selection and improve matching accuracy. Through extensive experiments on a range of public benchmark datasets, it is verified that FGCM-Net not only delivers competitive performance but also outperforms current state-of-the-art techniques, yielding new perspectives for person re-identification studies.

title: Structural Re-Parameterization Enhanced Object Detection for High-Precision PCB Defect Inspection in Industrial Manufacturing
authors: Jiaqi Liu
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Printed circuit board manufacturing demands automated defect detection systems capable of identifying subtle anomalies in real-time while maintaining high precision to minimize false alarms. This paper proposes a structural reparameterization enhanced object detection framework that decouples training-time feature learning from inference-time computational efficiency. During training, multi-branch convolutions with heterogeneous kernel sizes (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times 3,\ 1 \times 1$</tex>, identity) enrich gradient flow and enhance discriminative capacity for finegrained defect localization. At inference, these branches are algebraically merged into a single <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times 3$</tex> convolution, eliminating batch normalization overhead and reducing memory access costs. Extensive experiments on a PCB defect dataset comprising six categories demonstrate that our method achieves 94.7% mAP @ 0.5 with 4.8 ms inference latency (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$640 \times 640$</tex> input, batch=1, RTX 3090 GPU), false positive rate 3.8%, and false negative rate 4.2%, outperforming YOLOv8s by 1.2% mAP while maintaining realtime capability suitable for edge deployment. Ablation studies confirm that re-parameterization contributes +2.6% mAP improvement with negligible computational penalty, validating its effectiveness for industrial quality control applications.

title: A Proposed PV Generation System With Short-Term Prediction Integrated Battery Energy Storage System
authors: Achara Pichetjamroen, Natthawuth Somakettarin, Nithiphat Teerakawanich
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on Power Science and Engineering (ICPSE)
abstract: An effective PV generation system concept should integrate with the power control scheme to obtain the maximum power point operation and power ramp-rate can be controlled under 10%. This study presents an overall technology to achieve high efficiency PV generation system by including the short-term prediction system to notify the sun-coverage event 1–2 minutes in advance with machine learning and the image processing technique. The dc data logger with multi-sampling technique is employed to optimize the data storage for the recorder and categorize the input data, it can shorten time consuming to process all data. Experimental set-up for PV charger to Lithium NMC battery pack is evaluated the effectiveness of the PV system. The results confirm that a pack of batteries can be charged under MPPT and CV (Constant voltage) stage at 80% SOC and from 0% SOC until fully charged.

title: SCOPE: Satellite Communication Security Analysis and Mitigation Strategies Using STRIDE and Data Flow Diagrams
authors: Joy Wang, Rand Ashour, Md Ismail Hossain, Ragib Hasan
abstract: Satellite communication (SatCom) systems are critical for global connectivity, supporting broadband, navigation, remote sensing, and emergency services. However, many SatCom infrastructures were designed with minimal cybersecurity considerations, making them vulnerable to attacks such as spoofing and tampering. This paper introduces SCOPE, a structured framework for threat modeling and security analysis of SatCom systems using STRIDE and Data Flow Diagrams (DFDs). SCOPE models ground, link, and space segments, identifies segmentspecific threats, and proposes practical mitigation strategies aligned with operational constraints. To validate the framework, we implement controlled simulations of spoofing and tampering attacks and evaluate detection-based mitigation strategies. Results show that SCOPE significantly reduces attack success rates while maintaining minimal overhead, thereby offering a reusable methodology for improving SatCom security.

title: Risk-Adaptive Authorization for Agentic AI Systems
authors: Badal Bhushan, Karthik Pappu, VasanthRao Jadav, Nilesh Jaiswal
abstract: Agentic AI systems deploy autonomous agents that execute transactions with delegated authority, yet existing authorization mechanisms rely on static role-based policies that are inadequate for contexts varying in risk. Authentication protocols for ephemeral agents include SPIFFE, OAuth Token Exchange, and DPoP, addressing the question of “who is the agent?” This paper extends that foundation to address “what should the agent be allowed to do?” We present an authorization flow combining workload identity (SPIFFE/SPIRE), token exchange (RFC 8693), policy evaluation (Open Policy Agent), AI-based risk scoring, sender-constrained tokens (DPoP), and CIBA-inspired human approval for high-risk transactions. The architecture implements a three-tier authorization model: Tier 1 transactions receive automatic approval, Tier 2 transactions require supervisor-agent approval, and Tier 3 transactions trigger human-in-the-loop verification. We validate the approach through a retail exchange scenario. Measurements across 120 authorization requests show cold-path latency of 32.7 ms (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S D=18.5 ~\text{ms}$</tex>) and warm-path latency of 4.7 ms (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{S D}=\mathbf{2. 3 m s}$</tex>), a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7} \times$</tex> speedup with token caching. Delegation narrowing enforcement blocks all invalid privilege escalation attempts across 20 test cases. The results indicate that risk-adaptive authorization adds acceptable overhead for agentic systems while enabling contextual policy decisions and human oversight for high-risk operations.

title: Enhancing OCT Image Classification of Macular Diseases Using a Self-Built Dataset and Advanced Neural Networks
authors: Baozhi Zhang, Benqian Liu, Jingyuan Dong, Hongquan Qu, Changan Liu, Ziyi Li
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: The global incidence of retinal diseases is rising continuously, and early accurate diagnosis is critical to mitigating irreversible visual impairment. Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) is the gold standard for retinal structural imaging, but manual interpretation faces limitations such as heavy reliance on expertise and subjective bias. To address this gap, this paper proposes a deep learning model integrating residual blocks and a channel attention mechanism for multi-class classification of macular disease OCT images. Utilizing a self-built clinical dataset for training and evaluation, the model achieves an overall accuracy of 91.46%, outperforming several widely used deep learning models (e.g., VGG16, ResNet34, MobileNetV2). The proposed model demonstrates consistent classification performance across DME, CNV, Drusen, and normal categories, as evidenced by high AUC values and confusion matrix analysis, providing reliable technical support for clinical auxiliary diagnosis and large-scale screening.

title: Governed Agentic AI for Software Platforms: A Reference Architecture for Safe Autonomy at Scale
authors: Sureshkumar Karuppuchamy
abstract: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have accelerated the emergence of agentic AI systems capable of reasoning, planning, and acting across software platforms [1], [9]. While such systems promise significant productivity and operational gains, unconstrained autonomy introduces substantial risks related to safety, reliability, security, regulatory compliance, and organizational trust-particularly in large, distributed enterprise environments [3], [11], [12]. These concerns are further amplified by emerging regulatory frameworks such as the European Union's Digital Services Act [5], which impose stricter requirements on transparency, accountability, and human oversight. This paper introduces a governance control plane for agentic AI systems in software platforms and empirically characterizes the trade-offs between autonomy, safety enforcement, and human oversight. The proposed architecture balances autonomous decision-making with deterministic policy enforcement and structured human control. The proposed architecture decomposes agent behavior into four controlled layers: (1) intent interpretation using LLMs [1], (2) policy-aware planning and decision logic informed by enterprise rules and risk constraints [6]–[8], (3) guarded execution over platform APIs inspired by tool-augmented LLM paradigms [9], [10], and (4) continuous telemetry, auditing, and rollback mechanisms to ensure operational safety and accountability. Central to the architecture is the concept of bounded autonomy, where agents automate routine and low-risk decisions while remaining constrained by declarative policies, approval gates, runtime validation, and execution allow-lists. We demonstrate the applicability of this approach through representative enterprise scenarios, including AI-assisted feature rollout and automated issue triage, building on established software engineering and DevOps principles [2], [4]. Beyond qualitative analysis, we report prototype-based measurements of governance overhead and safety effectiveness, including policy-evaluation latency, invalid-action blocking rates, and changes in human intervention volume across autonomy tiers. We further discuss adversarial robustness considerations such as indirect prompt injection [11], [12], concurrency and state-drift management in long-running agents, and policy conflict resolution in multi-agent settings [13]. Overall, this work contributes a practical, governance-centered blueprint for deploying agentic AI systems with explicitly bounded autonomy, observable decision-making, and enforceable safety controls in large-scale software platforms.

title: Integrating Multi-Neural DAE Experts into Power System Dynamic Component Modeling for EMTP-Type Simulation
authors: Xu Zhou, Shilin Gao, Qiliang Jiang, Yuhong Wang, Lai Yuan, Zongsheng Zheng, Chenyu Zhou, Fang Gao
abstract: Traditional data-driven modeling methods encounter significant accuracy limitations when used to model the electromagnetic transients (EMT) of unidentified components in power systems based on limited measurement data. Moreover, their restricted generalization capabilities and compatibility issues further impede broad adoption. This paper proposes a novel multi-gate mixture-of-experts based differential-algebraic equation (MMOE-DAE) network to model components with unknown high-order dynamics. First, an MMOE-DAE network is established to model the relationships among the phases of the three-phase component by modulating multiple gated neural DAEs. Then, an automatic order upgrading module for high-order dynamic component modeling is proposed, and the identification of DAE order is facilitated by a fully gated network. Last, a model local parameter flexible calibration strategy based on fine-tuning is designed to improve the accuracy and stability of the MMOE-DAE training. Based on the commercial EMT simulation platform CloudPSS, dynamic equivalent models for different power components are constructed, and the accuracy and flexibility advantages of MMOE-DAE modeling are assessed through co-simulations.

title: Two-Stage Framework for Efficient UAV-Based Wildfire Video Analysis with Adaptive Compression and Fire Source Detection
authors: Yanbing Bai, Rui-Yang Ju, Lemeng Zhao, Junjie Hu, Jianchao Bi, Erick Mas, Shunichi Koshimura
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) have become increasingly important in disaster emergency response by facilitating aerial video analysis. Due to the limited computational resources available on UAVs, large models cannot be run efficiently for on-board analysis. To overcome this challenge, we propose a lightweight and efficient two-stage framework for wildfire monitoring and fire source detection on UAV platforms. Specifically, in Stage 1, we utilize a policy network to identify and discard redundant video clips, thereby reducing computational costs. We also introduce a station point mechanism that incorporates future frame information within the sequential policy network to improve prediction accuracy. This mechanism allows Stage 1 to operate in a near-real-time manner. In Stage 2, for frames classified as containing fire, we apply an improved YOLOv8 model to accurately localize the fire source in real-time on selected frames. We evaluate Stage 1 using the FLAME and HMDB51 datasets, and Stage 2 using the Fire & Smoke Detection Dataset. Experimental results show that our method significantly reduces computational costs while maintaining classification accuracy in Stage 1, and achieves high detection accuracy with real-time inference in Stage 2.

title: Software-Defined Reconfigurable Underwater Wireless Optical Communication System with Laser Array Modulation and Deep Learning Demodulation
authors: Tianchen Tong, Guanjun Gao, Jialiang Zhang, Ziqi Ma, Yonggang Guo, Shanguo Huang
abstract: Underwater wireless optical communication (UWOC) systems face an important trade-off between transmission rate and link robustness, which is further exacerbated by channel attenuation and the nonlinearity of opto-electronic conversion. To address these challenges, we propose a software-defined reconfigurable UWOC (SDR-UWOC) system based on laser diode (LD) arrays for optical superimposition without complex electrical digital-to-analog converters (DACs). The system employs a unified hardware platform that supports dynamic modulation and data rate switching between power-superimposed on-off keying (OOK) for robust communication and signal-superimposed pulse amplitude modulation with four levels (PAM4) for high-speed transmission. To mitigate optical device nonlinearities and channel impairments, we develop a hybrid convolutional neural network and long short-term memory (CNN-LSTM) demodulation scheme that combines spatial feature extraction with temporal sequence modeling. Experimental validation over a 1-meter water channel with controlled attenuation demonstrates that OOK superimposition improves attenuation tolerance by 3.1 dB compared to single-LD operation.
