The base heuristic achieved approximately 86 % of the utility of the greedy approach (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.78 \times 10^{20}$</tex> vs. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.81 \times 10^{20}$</tex>) while including only 23 transactions. The extended version with greedy fill reached up to 120 % of the reference utility (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.73 \times 10^{21}$</tex>), incorporating 268 transactions compared to 212 for the greedy, while maintaining execution times below 2 seconds. These preliminary results demonstrate the feasibility of capturing additional semantic value within time windows compatible with Ethereum block-building intervals, based on isolated experiments with bounded transaction sets.

title: Impacts of Incorporating End-User Behavior Into Demand Side Management Programs
authors: Isidora González V., Patricio A. Mendoza-Araya
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: The energy industry is responsible for a significant portion of carbon emissions. Considering the percentage of the population with poor access to energy, it is essential to move towards a just energy transition. This study evaluates the impact of considering user needs, as represented by behavioral economics, in Time of Use (ToU) and Real Time Pricing (RTP) models, to analyze whether this integration contributes to improvements in Demand Side Management (DSM) programs so that it becomes an effective tool in addressing current issues. The results show that including user diversity improves demand stability, increases distributor profits by about 38 % for RTP, and reduces them by about 10 % for ToU, compared to models without segmentation. This reinforces the importance of considering consumer preferences and responses in tariff design.

title: Tracking and Erosion Resistant Cellulose-Silica Co-doped Silicone Rubber Composites for Oil Filled Transformer Bushing Applications
authors: Jatoth Varun, Palash Mishra, Pawel Rozga, Tariq Nazir, Mithun Mondal, R. Sarathi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Silica doped room temperature vulcanized (RTV) silicone rubber microcomposites are widely used as surface coatings for porcelain based transformer bushings due to their excellent hydrophobicity and anti-pollution performance. However, prolonged exposure to transformer oil degrades these coatings making them susceptible tracking and erosion. Incorporation of cellulose fillers suppresses oil penetration by creating physical entanglements and tortuous diffusion pathways within the silicone rubber matrix. In this context, the present study investigates cellulose-silica co-doped RTV silicone rubber composites designed to mitigate tracking after aging in natural ester oil. Composite formulations were prepared with a fixed silica microparticle content of 30 wt.% and varying micro size cellulose loadings of 10 wt.%, 15 wt.% and 20 wt.% along with unfilled silicone rubber as a reference. Accelerated aging was carried out in natural ester oil at 80 °C for 200 hours. Post aging mass analysis and Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy revealed a progressive reduction in oil absorption and chain scission in both the silicone rubber backbone (Si-O-Si) and side chains (Si-CH₃) as the cellulose content increased from 10 to 15 wt.%. The composite containing 15 wt.% cellulose exhibited the lowest weight gain and minimal chemical degradation. Thermogravimetric analysis and pyrolysis studies further demonstrated enhanced residual mass and smoother pyrolyzed surfaces with fewer cracks indicating improved thermal stability after aging. IEC 60587 inclined plane tracking and erosion testing confirmed that the aged composite co-doped with 30 wt.% SiO₂ and 15 wt.% cellulose successfully passed the test, demonstrating its suitability for oil-exposed transformer bushing insulation applications.

title: BLADE: Breast Lesion Analysis with Domain Expertise for DCE-MRI Diagnosis_supp1-3684147.pdf
authors: Zhenwei Shi
abstract: Dynamic Contrast-Enhanced Magnetic Reso nance Imaging (DCE-MRI) is pivotal in breast cancer diag nosis, yet radiologists face challenges in interpreting its complex data due to the lack of robust automated tools. Current lesion diagnosis systems struggle with limited datasets and insufficient integration of domain knowledge. To overcome these limitations, we propose Breast Lesion Analysis with DomainExpertise(BLADE),anoveldiagnosis framework that synergizes deep learning with clinical ex pertise. BLADE leverages a pre-trained vertical foundation model (optimized via Momentum Contrast on 2.1 million MRI slices) as its encoder, ensuring robust feature extraction. Crucially, the system incorporates prior multi-phasic hemodynamic knowledge to emulate radiologists' diagnos tic reasoning and introduces a Breast Imaging Reporting and Data System (BI-RADS)-based constraint during training to align predictions with clinical standards. Extensive experiments demonstrate that BLADE outperforms state of-the-art methods, achieving an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.9228 and 0.9553 on two external test datasets, respectively. Notably, BLADE significantly enhances clin ical workflow; when used as an assistive tool, BLADE improves diagnostic accuracy by 14.31%, surpassing stan daloneperformanceofclinicians. This workbridgesthegap between AI-driven analysis and clinical practice in breast MRI interpretation. The source code is available at https: //github.com/GDPHMediaLab/BLADE.

title: Characterizations of A Double-Sided Multi-Chip SiC Power Module with Interleaved Layouts
authors: Yongqi Pei, Gaojia Zhu, Lu Wang, Wenbin Deng, Longnv Li, Yun-Hui Mei
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: To meet the demanding requirements of high-performance and high-reliability silicon carbide (SiC) power modules in high-power-density motor controllers for electric vehicles, this paper proposes a double-sided double-interleaved (DSDI) multi-chip SiC module package and establishes a systematic electro-thermal-mechanical co-design and evaluation methodology. Electrically, the shorter conduction path in the power loop of the DSDI structure, combined with a symmetrical interleaved layout, significantly reduces parasitic inductance (4.87 nH measured) and resistance (4.36 mΩ), while improving dynamic current sharing. Thermally, a double-sided cooling structure with suppressed thermal coupling achieves a thermal resistance as low as 0.0855 K/W, resulting in a reduction of the junction temperature by over 10.85% compared to the traditional double-sided package. For reliability, spacers are optimally designed to mitigate thermo-mechanical stress. The Anand constitutive model, coupled with the Coffin-Manson fatigue life prediction model, provides a theoretical basis for fatigue life assessment under power cycling. A fabricated 1700-V, 400-A DSDI prototype module demonstrates excellent overall performance and reliability, validating the proposed multi-physics integrated design approach.

title: Fault-Tolerant Hall Position Signal Processing Method for Symmetric Winding Multi-Phase Brushless DC Motor Systems
authors: Wei Chen, Rongkai Li, Shi Zhang, Lixiang Zhu, Kexin Song, Xinmin Li
abstract: Hall-effect sensors are essential for commutation and rotor position detection in multi-phase brushless DC motor (BLDCM) systems. However, as the number of phases increases, more sensors are required, leading to a higher fault probability and increased risk of commutation errors. To address this, a fault-tolerant Hall signal processing method is proposed for multi-phase BLDCM systems. The discrete Hall signals are transformed into a continuous rotational vector in the αβ stationary frame, fully exploiting the spatial redundancy of multi-phase signals to suppress high-order harmonics. Harmonic decomposition and filtering further refine the signals and provide smooth quadrature inputs for a quadrature phase-locked loop (QPLL) to estimate rotor position in real time. A fault-tolerant harmonic decoupling (FT-HD) module is then introduced to compensate for DC offsets and harmonics caused by sensor faults without altering the filter structure. Experiments on a nine-phase BLDCM verify that the proposed method ensures accurate position estimation under single-sensor faults and significantly reduces torque and current ripples.

title: Confounder Balance in Next Basket Prediction
authors: Zhiying Deng, Jianjun Li, Wei Liu, Juan Zhao
abstract: Next basket prediction (NBP) is essential for online businesses such as grocery shopping and retail, as it aims to learn users’ interests based on historical basket sequences and predict a set of items for the next purchase. Existing methods tend to prioritize items with high interaction frequencies in the dataset. In this article, we use causal analysis to theoretically demonstrate that such methods can lead to biased results. Specifically, item interactions in the dataset represent item-specific weights that reflect average user preferences. However, these weights are imprecise due to varying user interest levels, leading to biased learning results and suboptimal predictions. We find that repeated interactions between a user and items can represent the user’s level of interest, which can be leveraged to improve user interest modeling by assigning user-specific weights. In addition, different users exhibit varying preferences for items with high interaction frequencies, highlighting the necessity of different degrees of bias mitigation. Making personalized adjustments based on these differences can further refine existing methods. Consequently, we propose a simple yet effective confounder balance prediction (CBP) model to mitigate bias while preserving individual user interests. Specifically, we employ counterfactual inference to construct a counterfactual world in which predictions are influenced solely by either high interaction frequencies or user interests, both of which are potential confounders. This approach enables us to individually assess the impact of these confounders on interaction probabilities. The goal of CBP is to balance the confounders and thereby refine the learning process without resorting to oversimplified unbiased learning. Experiments on four widely used real-world datasets demonstrate the significant advantages of CBP over existing state-of-the-art methods. Our code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/Hiiizhy/CBP</uri>

title: Effect of Lightweight Design on Structural Dynamics and Actuation Energy Demand in Hydraulically Actuated Flexible Systems
authors: Tirtha Gupta, Aleksi Härkönen, Dominik Walica, Qasim Khadim, Antti Ahola, Emil Kurvinen
abstract: Growing demands for productivity, environmental sustainability, energy efficiency, and automation are reshaping the lightweight design of hydraulically actuated heavy machinery. Lightweight structures are often evaluated by static assessments such as deformation and stress, addressing minimal consideration to structural dynamics and hydraulic energy consumption. This study introduces a workflow for lightweight and energy-efficient design of hydraulically actuated systems. The workflow utilizes Eurocode constrained structural optimization and flexible multibody dynamic simulation to evaluate design performance. The framework aims to reduce structural weight of a lift boom through cross-sectional design of steel grades ranging from yield strength of 460-1100 MPa when only in-plane loading was considered. Both default and optimized lift boom were first compared through linear structural analysis to obtain deformation, stresses, natural frequencies and mode shapes. The system’s actuator mechanical work and dynamic performance were then evaluated by flexible multibody dynamic simulation through a simulated work cycle. Lightweighting of the lift boom through the proposed workflow yielded 21.9% weight reduction, with tip deflection increased by 67% and the first mode frequency reduced by 39.3% in the horizontal direction. Dynamic simulation demonstrated that the optimized lift boom required less mechanical work while maintaining similar dynamic response under identical valve command. The proposed workflow addresses current challenges in lightweight design for hydraulically actuated systems and incorporates dynamic simulation feedback to support the evolution of smarter, energy-efficient heavy machinery.

title: Rock-Physics-Guided Seismic Estimation of Elastic Modulus Dispersion for Microcrack Identification in Tight Sandstones
authors: Danyu Zhao, Cai Liu, Zhiqi Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate characterization of microcracks is essential for predicting favorable zones in tight sandstone reservoirs. However, their complex seismic rock physics responses pose significant challenges for mapping microcrack distribution. This study presents a rock-physics-guided framework to estimate elastic modulus dispersion parameters from pre-stack seismic data for microcrack characterization. Log-scale rock physics modeling is integrated as prior information into seismic-scale inversion to ensure physically consistent estimation of frequency-dependent elastic parameters. Dispersion effects of bulk and shear moduli associated with microcracks are quantified using the equivalent inclusion-average stress (EIAS) model. A model-driven rock physics inversion scheme is then developed to estimate microcrack properties and dispersion parameters from well logs. Both theoretical modeling and log-based inversion indicate strong positive correlations between dispersion parameters and microcrack density, with shear modulus dispersion showing a more robust relationship. Log-derived frequency-dependent elastic impedance (FEI) serves as rock-physics-based priors for seismic FEI inversion. The inversion employs an adjoint-state-accelerated gradient-based optimization using the limited-memory Broyden-Fletcher-Goldfarb-Shanno (L-BFGS) algorithm with time-frequency-angle-domain forward modeling. The inverted FEI volumes are subsequently converted into frequency-dependent bulk and shear moduli, from which dispersion parameters are estimated to evaluate microcrack development. Synthetic tests demonstrate that the method reliably recovers dispersion parameters consistent with log-derived microcrack density. Field applications further show that the framework achieves improved resolution and reliability compared with amplitude-versus-offset (AVO) and frequency-dependent AVO (FAVO) inversions. Overall, this study offers a physically consistent and practical strategy for estimating elastic modulus dispersion, thereby improving microcrack characterization in tight sandstone reservoirs.

title: A Cloud-Native Alternative Correction for Landsat-8/9 Collection 2 Surface Reflectance over Inland Waters_supp1-3684904.docx
authors: Kun Shi
abstract: The Landsat Collection 2 (C2) archive is vital for inland water monitoring, yet the Land Surface Reflectance Code (LaSRC) atmospheric correction for Landsat-8/9 introduces Dark Plume Over Water (DPOW) artifacts. These aerosol extrapolation errors cause severe negative biases in shortwave bands, disrupting long-term consistency. To address this, we developed a cloud-native Alternative Correction (AC) method on Google Earth Engine. This data-driven approach employs random forest regression, trained on spatiotemporally aggregated high-quality water pixels, to reconstruct reliable surface reflectance (SR) from Top-of-Atmosphere observations. Validation against a global in-situ hyperspectral dataset and benchmarking against the physics-based ACOLITE processor demonstrate the robustness of the proposed method. While ACOLITE effectively resolves the negative bias issue, the AC method achieves superior radiometric accuracy, reducing the ultra-blue Root Mean Square Error to 0.019 (compared to 0.029 for ACOLITE and 0.031 for C2 SR). Notably, under high-aerosol conditions, the AC method minimizes the residual spectral distortions often observed in physical inversions, effectively restoring the natural spectral shape. Spatially, the method eliminates DPOW artifacts; furthermore, it removes systematic biases between Landsat-8/9 and legacy sensors (Landsat-4/5/7). By restoring radiometric integrity, this automated solution secures the foundation for reliable long-term global limnology.

title: SatFedGuard: Semi-Supervised Federated Contrastive Learning with RL-Assisted Bidirectional Distillation for Anomaly Traffic Detection in Satellite Networks
authors: Yu Gu, Le Zhang, Yunyi Zhang, Ye Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Federated learning-based intrusion detection methods for satellite networks enable model training without sharing local data, thereby ensuring network security while significantly reducing communication overhead. However, due to the difficulty of obtaining large-scale high-quality labeled data in satellite environments, a key challenge lies in how to train intrusion detection models using abundant unlabeled traffic data. We propose SatFedGuard, a semi-supervised federated contrastive learning approach for anomaly traffic detection in satellite networks. SatFedGuard effectively integrates unlabeled in-orbit data with labeled data from ground stations for model training. First, it models the unlabeled satellite traffic data using a contrastive learning framework. To address the challenge of non-IID data distribution, an attention-based dual-path aggregation strategy is designed to generate personalized models for each satellite by leveraging model similarities. Then, a bidirectional multi-granularity distillation method between larger and smaller models is implemented, where reinforcement learning is employed to optimize the weights of different loss terms dynamically. Experiments on two satellite network traffic datasets under non-IID settings demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves anomaly detection performance while reducing dependence on in-orbit labeled data, achieving F1-Scores of 93.38% (↑11.63%) and 99.80% (↑8.72%), respectively.

title: SLAB: A Self-supervised Label Generation Framework to Reduce Annotation Overhead
authors: Kshitish Ghate, Hrithik Nambiar, Alfiya M. Shaikh, Swarnali Banik, Surjya Ghosh, Niloy Ganguly, Snehanshu Saha
abstract: Recently, many emotion-aware services have been designed to improve the user experience during online video consumption (e.g., online meeting platforms, and online tutoring mediums). These services primarily deploy a machine learning model that infers user emotion continuously based on the variation in different physiological signals . However, the major challenge to developing such models is to continuously ask the users to self-report their emotions for ground truth annotation. To address these challenges, we present SLAB (Self-supervised LABel generator), a self-supervised deep learning framework for label generation to reduce the tabular data annotation overhead. SLAB utilizes two pretext tasks coupled with a novel <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$log-cosh$</tex-math></inline-formula> loss function to learn useful representations from unlabeled data that apply to the downstream task of label generation. SLAB outperforms supervised learning, semi-supervised learning, and self-supervised learning baselines such as Value Imputation and Mask Estimation (VIME) on continuous emotion annotation datasets - Continuously Annotated Signals of Emotion (CASE) and K-EmoCon. We also demonstrate the generalizability of the SLAB on a unique wheelchair surface vibration dataset (in addition to four publicly available datasets from different domains). These findings highlight that SLAB significantly reduces the annotation overhead in situations where the number of unlabeled samples vastly outnumbers labeled samples.

title: Complex AI2AI Adoption Intention in Service Organisations
authors: Nikolaos Pappas, Anna Farmaki, Dimitrios Stergiou, Christina Karadimitriou
abstract: AI2AI holds great potential as a technological innovation that may significantly change business operations. However, AI2AI adoption intentions remain under-researched in business contexts. This study addresses this gap by examining service managers' intentions to adopt AI2AI through the lens of complexity theory. Drawing on survey data from 400 Greek hotel managers, the study investigates how combinations of organisational and contextual factors shape AI2AI adoption intention. Fuzzy-set Qualitative Comparative Analysis (fsQCA) identifies sufficient configurational pathways, while Necessary Condition Analysis (NCA) determines constraining conditions. Robustness is assessed through alternative data-cleaning procedures and sensitivity analyses across calibration thresholds and consistency cut-offs. The analysis identifies four configurational pathways to AI2AI adoption (beneficial impacts; supported innovativeness; benefit–barrier nexus; competitiveness-driven adoption). Follow-up semi-structured interviews with industry stakeholders provide contextual corroboration and interpretive insight into how these adoption logics are understood and evaluated in practice. The findings highlight the complex and non-linear nature of AI2AI adoption intentions and offer theoretical and practical implications.

title: Two-Dimensional Central DOA Estimation for Incoherently Distributed Sources in Unknown Nonuniform Noise
authors: He Xu, Ming Jin, Qinghua Guo, Hua Chen
abstract: Most existing direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation methods are presented for a point source model, which could suffer from substantial performance degradation in multipath transmission scenarios. Although there have been some reports on DOA estimation in the case of multipath transmission or a distributed source model, they all rely on the prior knowledge of the number of sources under the assumption of uniform noise, which may not be available or satisfied in practice. This paper proposes two efficient two-dimensional (2-D) DOA estimation methods for incoherently distributed (ID) sources in unknown nonuniform noise, building on the generalized array manifold (GAM) of an L-shaped array and array covariance and cross covariance vectors. In particular, the first method employs a conjugate symmetry operation to enlarge the array aperture and two 1-D subspace spectral searches to estimate 2-D central DOAs independently. The second method constructs a sparse total least squares (STLS) problem to handle the impact of model bias and finite number of samples, and applies the alternating descent algorithm to achieve an improved 2-D central DOA estimation without knowing the number of sources. Finally, a simple and effective parameter pairing scheme is designed to avoid the ambiguity problem. Simulation results are provided to validate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed methods.

title: Resilient Distributed Filtering Under Data-Importance-Aware Attacks via Adaptive Quantization Encoding
authors: Zhidong Zhou, Jiabiao Huang, Shoucheng Yuan, Jun Cheng, Xianfu Wu, Jinde Cao
abstract: This paper investigates the resilient distributed filtering problem for networked Markov jump systems with time-varying delays under bandwidth constraints. In practical Internet of Things environments, limited communication resources and adversarial disruptions can jointly degrade estimation performance. To explicitly handle the trade-off between communication efficiency and estimation accuracy under adversarial conditions, a communication-aware, data-importance-driven encoding mechanism is proposed. By dynamically adjusting the quantization density and range according to real-time measurement importance, the proposed mechanism gives higher transmission priority to critical information, thereby improving the effective signal-to-noise ratio without increasing the overall communication burden. Moreover, an attack-dependent distributed filtering scheme is developed to counter data-importance-aware denial-of-service attacks, where the filter gains are adaptively reconfigured according to the attacked sensor nodes and system modes. Numerical simulations and a pulse-width-modulation-driven boost converter example verify the effectiveness of the proposed approach. In particular, compared with existing methods, the proposed mechanism improves the mean signal-to-noise ratio by 12 dB and reduces the average bandwidth consumption by 25% (3 bits/transmission versus 4 bits/transmission), while still maintaining a low estimation error (MSE = 0.0034) under targeted DoS attacks.

title: When Generative AI Meets Industry 5.0: Human-Centered AIGC Service Provision for Cyber-Physical Manufacturing
authors: Long Zhang, Wenjing Li, Hongliang Zhang, Xiaobo Zhou, Anming Dong, Dusit Niyato, Zhu Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Integrating Generative Artificial Intelligence (GAI) into Industry 5.0 presents transformative potential for Cyber-Physical Manufacturing (CPM) systems, enabling the human-machine interactive operations with higher degree of efficiency and productivity. However, deploying Artificial Intelligence Generated Content (AIGC) services in the CPM systems is fraught with challenges, such as resource constraints, extensive model sizes, and stochastic task generation. Therefore, it is essential to provision AIGC services through a co-design of fine-tuning service association, fine-tuned model transmission, and resource allocation. Notably, Industry 5.0’s emphasis on human-centricity allows humans to experience a more smooth operational interaction with smart machines (SMs), which necessitates shortening the completion latency of AIGC tasks. In this paper, we propose a human-centered AIGC service provision scheme for jointly designing the AIGC service associations of fine-tuning and model transmission at the SMs and edge servers (ESs), as well as the bandwidth and transmit power allocations at the ESs. We target the complete workflow particularly with model finetuning, model transmission, and inference execution. On this basis, we formulate the AIGC service provision problem for average task completion latency minimization, and develop a hierarchical deep reinforcement learning algorithm to solve it. Specifically, the discrete action policy of AIGC service association is learned via Dueling Double Deep Q-Network (D3QN) in the first layer, to guide the training of the second layer that learns the continuous action policy of resource allocation by soft actor-critic (SAC). Moreover, to improve the training stability and accelerate convergence, curriculum learning is adopted during the training phase to progressively update the neural networks of both D3QN and SAC. Simulation results show that the proposed scheme outperforms benchmark schemes, achieving a significant reduction in average task completion latency.

title: Harmonic Principle-Based Adjustable Constant-Force Mechanism for Stable Human-Robot Interaction in Massage Robotics
authors: Lixing Jin, Xuanquan Wang, Xinchun Wang, Xingguang Duan, Li Zhang
abstract: Stable and precise force contact is critical in human-robot interaction, typically requiring precise sensors and advanced algorithms. This paper presents a novel adjustable constant-force mechanism (CFM) serving as a force generator for massage robotic systems, aiming to reduce hardware costs and system development complexity. The passive CFM, comprising gears and springs, is further integrated with an active motor to enable online adjustment and continuous operation. As the primary innovation, the harmonic principle is employed for force regulation in the CFM, replacing traditional methods such as altering the number or assembly position of springs, which provides a more convenient integration with automated systems. This involves the superposition of two elastic systems with identical configurations, where only the phase angle is extracted as the sole control variable for force modulation. A prototype was constructed to experimentally validate its performance. Results demonstrate that the proposed solution achieves a maximum constant output force of 18.34 N over a 55 mm range, and it can be adjusted online to the appropriate level as required by the task.

title: Enhancing the Performance of Event-Related Potential-Based Brain–Computer Interfaces under Cognitive Distraction: A Multiwindow Adaptive Approach
authors: Minju Kim, Dojin Heo, Jongsu Kim, Sung-Phil Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Event-related potential (ERP)-based brain– computer interfaces (BCIs) require focused attention to presented stimuli. However, their applications in real life frequently involve environments that demand multitasking and impose cognitive distraction. Such distractions degrade ERP amplitudes and consequently reduce BCI performance. This study proposes a multiwindow adaptive model to mitigate the adverse effects of cognitive distraction on visual ERP-based BCIs. The proposed approach divides poststimulus intervals into multiple overlapping windows, each with dedicated spatial filters and classifiers that continuously update through adaptive semi-supervised learning. Offline experiments on a BCI control dataset collected during concurrent speaking demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms single-window or fixed (i.e., nonadaptive) models, yielding an accuracy of 91.08%. Further validation in an online experiment confirms that the multiwindow adaptive approach effectively restores BCI performance, achieving an accuracy of 93.20% despite cognitive distraction. These findings highlight the practical benefits of temporally tailored feature extraction and continuous adaptation for real-world ERP-based BCIs, enabling robust performance even under cognitive distraction.

title: UPSEC: Unstable-Position-Synchronization based Erasure Code for High-Security Robust Steganography
authors: Kai Zeng, Kejiang Chen, Weiming Zhang, Jin Li, Yaofei Wang, Nenghai Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: People are accustomed to sharing images on online social networks (OSNs), which is suited for covert communication. Robust steganography can survive JPEG recompression from OSN but sacrifices considerable security. This paper endeavors to achieve high-security robust steganography, an issue that plagues the current field. We define truncation-affected coefficients as unstable coefficients; they are major sources of error during recompression.We find that the unstable coefficients remain unstable under repeated recompression, which means both sides of the steganographic communication can naturally synchronize their positions. Therefore, we propose the unstable position synchronization based erasure code (UPSEC), where we replace the value of the unstable coefficient with a constant and set the distortion of the unstable coefficient to infinity when embedding. The receiver locates the unstable coefficients and sets their values to the same constant before extraction. By UPSEC, the unstable coefficient is effectively erased, so recompression errors no longer influence message extraction. Therefore, UPSEC achieves improved security without compromising robustness. Experimental results show that UPSEC can improve security by about +14% when compared to the state-of-the-art method against SRNet.

title: Research on Hidden Danger Analysis Technology of Distribution System Based on Branch Voltage Feature Analysis
authors: Hongyi Yang, Jian Gao, Jingzhi Liu, Zhenghong Bao, Jianming Zhang, Quanlei Qu
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Under the condition of new energy access, the discharge of various power equipment is one of the main reasons for equipment failure and even electrical fires in distribution networks. Due to the diversity of source and load types and the complexity of circuit topology, the detection of discharge faults is more challenging. This paper proposes a method for detecting discharge faults using branch voltage signals. When a discharge fault occurs in the line, the branch voltage has obvious pulse peak characteristics compared with the normal state. After analyzing the influence of filters, wavelet transform and empirical mode decomposition on signal processing, wavelet transform is selected to extract the high-frequency components of the branch voltage, and then the sum of the absolute values of the component coefficients is used to characterize the line state, and a discharge fault detection algorithm is constructed based on this value.

title: From Social Engineering to Financial Fraud: A Multi-Agent LLM Approach to Vishing Detection
authors: Sherif Abdelhamid, James Bangura, Benjamin Davis
abstract: Voice phishing (vishing) attacks have become increasingly sophisticated, driven by large-scale call centers, social engineering scripts, and AI-generated synthetic voices, posing serious risks to individuals and organizations. Existing vishing detection approaches predominantly rely on monolithic machine learning or deep learning models that offer limited transparency and struggle to capture the multi-dimensional nature of fraudulent voice interactions. This paper presents a novel, web-based vishing detection platform (VishGuard) powered by a large language model (LLM)-based multi-agent system that provides accurate, explainable, and real-time detection across voice calls, emails, and text messages. The proposed architecture decomposes the detection task into six specialized agents, each focusing on a distinct dimension of fraudulent behavior, including product and service deception, linguistic threat intelligence, social engineering tactics, financial fraud indicators, and compliance and legal violations. A central evaluator aggregates evidence generated by these agents to produce a calibrated scam likelihood score and an interpretable rationale. The analytical agents are implemented using the OpenAI GPT-4o Mini model, while voice inputs are processed through automatic speech recognition (ASR) using the Google Gemini 2.5 Flash model. To evaluate the system, a balanced dataset of 3,000 records was constructed, consisting of safe and malicious voice messages and call transcripts. The dataset was primarily curated by domain experts, augmented with synthetically generated samples using GPT-5 and Claude Sonnet 4.5, and further expanded through paraphrasing, Synonym replacement, and audio noise injection. Experimental results demonstrate strong performance, achieving 97.25% accuracy, 99.48% precision, 95% recall, and 97.19% F1-score, while maintaining a low false-positive rate of 0.5%. These results indicate that the proposed multi-agent approach effectively balances detection sensitivity with usability and trust. Overall, this work advances vishing defense by introducing an explainable, modular, and deployment-ready AI system that addresses key limitations of existing ML-, DL-, and LLM-based detection methods.

title: PlotChain: Deterministic Checkpointed Evaluation of Multimodal LLMs on Engineering Plot Reading
authors: Mayank Ravishankara
abstract: We present PlotChain, a deterministic, generatorbased benchmark for evaluating multimodal large language models (MLLMs) on engineering plot reading-recovering quantitative values from classic plots (e.g., Bode/FFT, step response, stress-strain, pump curves) rather than OCR-only extraction or free-form captioning. PlotChain contains 15 plot families with 450 rendered plots (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0}$</tex> per family), where every item is produced from known parameters and paired with exact ground truth computed directly from the generating process. A central contribution is checkpoint-based diagnostic evaluation: in addition to final targets, each item includes intermediate “cp_*” fields that isolate subskills (e.g., reading cutoff frequency or peak magnitude) and enable failure localization within a plot family. We evaluate four state-of-the-art MLLMs under a standardized, deterministic protocol (temperature <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0$</tex> and a strict JSON-only numeric output schema) and score predictions using per-field tolerances designed to reflect human plot-reading precision. Under the plotread tolerance policy, the top models achieve 80.42 % (Gemini 2.5 Pro), 79.84 % (GPT-4.1), and 78.21 % (Claude Sonnet 4.5) overall field-level pass rates, while GPT-4o trails at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 1. 5 9 \%}$</tex>. Despite strong performance on many families, frequency-domain tasks remain brittle: bandpass response stays low (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 23 \%$</tex>), and FFT spectrum remains challenging. We release the generator, dataset, raw model outputs, scoring code, and manifests with checksums to support fully reproducible runs and retrospective rescoring under alternative tolerance policies.

title: Multi-Class Disease Identification in Cauliflower Plants Using YOLOv11 and DINOv2
authors: Viviana Moya, Juan Pablo Vásconez, A. Córdova T. Luis, Pablo Vaca F. Juan, William Chamorro, Andrea Pilco
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Automated disease recognition systems in agricultural yields are essential for sustainable farming practices, particularly for regions lacking basic early disease detection technology. Diagnosing various diseases affecting cauliflower plants needs immediate research because cauliflower serves as a crucial vegetable crop. The research aims to bridge the technological gap by proposing a deep learning-based framework that leverages YOLOv11 for real-time object detection and DINOv2 for robust image classification. The VegNet dataset consisted of four classes, including disease-free and bacterial spot and black rot and downy mildew, which served to train both models. DINOv2 operating with AdamW and a learning rate of 1e-5 during ten epochs reaches perfection in all metrics with scores of 1.00 and surpasses the best YOLOv11 configuration. The research comparison demonstrates that DINOv2 enables precise diagnoses at optimal speed while creating foundations for intelligent farming systems in underprivileged agricultural settings.

title: A Short Survey and Brief Tutorial on the State of the Art in Internet Routing Security
authors: Carlos Martínez Cagnazzo, Cristina Mayr, Claudio Risso
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This paper examines recent advancements in securing Internet routing, focusing on Resource Public Key Infrastructure/Route Origin Validation (RPKI/ROV) and revisiting established mechanisms like Internet Routing Registries (IRRs). We begin by briefly outlining Internet routing, with a particular emphasis on the Border Gateway Protocol (BGP). Following this introduction, we categorize common routing incidents, including leaks and hijacks. Finally, we delve into the techniques and protocols designed to mitigate these security threats, detailing the scenarios for which they are most suitable.

title: A System-Oriented Framework for Coexistence Testing of Multi-Radio Platforms in the 2.4 GHz Band
authors: Kiran Kumar Dhani Reddy
abstract: The increasing integration of multiple wireless technologies such as Zigbee, Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE), and Wi-Fi within a single embedded platform has enabled advanced connectivity in modern industrial automation and mining systems. These multi-radio platforms support diverse functions ranging from safety-critical control to high-throughput telemetry and diagnostics. However, the shared use of the congested 2.4 GHz ISM band introduces complex coexistence challenges that are not sufficiently addressed by conventional regulatory compliance testing, which primarily focuses on transmitter characteristics and spectral mask requirements. This paper proposes a system-oriented framework for coexistence testing of multi-radio platforms operating in the 2.4 GHz band, with a specific focus on heavy machinery and autonomous mining applications. The framework extends beyond traditional packet error rate (PER) analysis by incorporating latency, jitter, recovery time, and control-loop stability as key performance indicators for mission-critical and safety-critical systems. A layered test methodology is introduced that combines controlled RF interference, protocol-level traffic stress, and EMC-induced disturbances to emulate real-world industrial operating conditions. Experimental observations from representative multi-radio configurations demonstrate that coexistence-induced failures often originate from nonobvious coupling paths, including shared power distribution networks, digital interfaces, antenna near-field interactions, and cable re-radiation from large metallic structures. The proposed framework enables repeatable, scalable, and technologyagnostic coexistence validation, bridging the gap between regulatory testing and real-world system reliability requirements. Design and validation guidelines are provided to support robust multi-radio integration in harsh industrial environments.

title: A Continuous-Time Auto-Ranging Interface for Wide-Dynamic-Range Resistive Sensing
authors: Faisal Alamri, Hussian Maanaki, Ali Farahani, Hossein Niakan, Jun Wang, Babak Parkhideh
abstract: In this paper a low-cost, portable resistance measurement instrument capable of continuous, high-accuracy operation over a wide resistance range from 50Ω to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$40 \mathrm{M} \Omega$</tex> is detailed and presented. The proposed architecture employs a voltage divider based measurement architecture with selectable reference resistors and a dynamic range-switching algorithm to enable auto range selection across multiple resistance decades. Experimental results demonstrate a relative measurement error below 2% throughout a wide dynamic measurement range, while the proposed switching strategy ensures stable range transitions with minimal transient disturbance and measurement discontinuities. The developed instrument is suitable for applications that require precise and continuous resistance monitoring under conditions of significant resistance variability.

title: Comparative Study of DTC and PTC with Constrained Voltage Vectors in Multiphase Drive
authors: Esteban Leguizamón, Paola Maidana, Christian Medina, Jorge Rodas, Osvaldo Gonzalez, Gustavo Ojeda, Amabilis Hernandez
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Various control strategies have been proposed for six-phase induction machines to exploit their inherent benefits, including phase redundancy, enhanced torque density, and fault tolerance. Among these strategies, Direct Torque Control (DTC) and Predictive Torque Control (PTC) in their classic forms stand out due to their implementation feasibility and capacity to optimise dynamic performance. This study presents a comparative analysis of both methods using constrained voltage vectors to suppress currents in the (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x-y$</tex>) plane. The assessment relies on two figures of merit: Mean Square Error, for tracking accuracy of torque, flux, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(x-y)$</tex> currents; and total harmonic distortion for harmonic analysis of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex> current. The results highlight the trade-off between computational complexity and dynamic response fidelity, and offer practical technical guidance for selecting control strategies in multiphase drive applications.

title: A Contrastive Learning-Based Unknown Attack Detection Method
authors: Zhixin Xia, Tao Wang, Xiaohui Wu
publish: 2025 2nd World Conference on Computer and Information Security (WCCIS)
abstract: Detecting previously unseen network attacks remains a persistent challenge for intrusion detection systems (IDS). Many traditional machine learning approaches have difficulty adapting to evolving threats and rely heavily on handcrafted features, which limits their ability to identify new attack patterns. While deep learning has improved detection accuracy, many existing methods overlook the alignment between learned deep features and the intended clustering objective, which can reduce effectiveness. In this work, we present a Contrastive Learning-based Unknown Attack Detection (CL-UAD) approach that combines deep feature extraction with a clustering mechanism guided by semantic text descriptions. The model encourages features of the same category to cluster tightly while pushing apart those of different categories, and also incorporates a sample-wise contrastive term to enhance robustness against variations in network traffic data. During evaluation on UNSWNB15 and CIC-IDS2017 datasets, CL-UAD consistently showed improved detection rates for various unseen attack types compared with existing deep learning baselines. The approach demonstrates potential for deployment in environments where the nature of attacks changes over time.

title: The GenAI Generation: Student Views of Awareness, Preparedness, and Concern
authors: Micaela Siraj, Jon Duke, Thomas Plötz
abstract: Generative Artificial Intelligence (Gen AI) is revolutionizing education and workforce development, profoundly shaping how students learn, engage, and prepare for their future. Outpacing the development of uniform policies and structures, Gen AI has heralded a unique era and given rise to the Gen AI Generation, a cohort of individuals whose development has been increasingly shaped by the opportunities and challenges Gen AI presents during its widespread adoption within society. This study examines higher education students' perceptions of Gen AI through a concise survey with optional open-ended questions, focusing on their awareness, preparedness, and concerns. “Readiness” appears increasingly tied to exposure to Gen AI through one's coursework. Students with greater curricular exposure to Gen AI tend to feel more prepared, while those without more often express vulnerability and uncertainty, highlighting a new and growing divide that goes beyond traditional disciplinary boundaries. Evaluation of more than 250 responses, with over 40 percent providing detailed qualitative feedback, reveals a core dual sentiment: while most students express enthusiasm for Gen AI, an even greater proportion voice a spectrum of concerns about ethics, job displacement, and the adequacy of educational structures given the highly transformative technology. These findings offer critical insights into how students view the potential and pitfalls of Gen AI for future career impacts.

title: Research on Low-Altitude Small Target Detection Method Based on YOLO11x-ESO
authors: Chuang Xu, Fang Wang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: This paper addresses the detection requirements for densely distributed small targets, such as pedestrians, vehicles, motorcycles, and boats, in urban low-altitude aerial photography scenarios. It proposes a low-altitude multi-object detection algorithm based on the YOLO11x framework. Targets in low-altitude scenes are significantly affected by viewing angle, scale, and background interference. Especially when target sizes are smaller than 20 pixels, the feature representation capability of conventional detection models decreases significantly. To address these challenges, this paper introduces a multi-level attention mechanism, CBAM (Convolutional Block Attention Module), into the basic YOLO structure to enhance the model's ability to represent fine-grained target textures and edge information. Furthermore, an additional high-resolution detection layer is added to the feature fusion and detection head parts to improve the localization accuracy of extremely small targets. Experimental results indicate that this method achieves better performance in terms of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex>, recall rate for small targets, and detection performance in complex dense scenes compared to the basic YOLO model. The proposed model improves detection accuracy in scenarios such as low-altitude intelligent perception, urban supervision, and traffic monitoring.

title: Predictive Modeling of the COVID-19 Reproduction Number Using Auto-ARIMA and Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Pastor E Pérez-Estigarribia, Ayrton Giménez, Diego Stalder, Hyun Ho Shin, Carlos Sauer, Sebastián Grillo
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This study evaluates the Auto-ARIMA model with exogenous variables to predict the COVID-19 reproduction number (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R_{e}$</tex>), comparing it with multivariate regressor models based on Lasso, Extra Trees, KNN and Huber loss. As a case study, data from Paraguay were used, analyzing different pandemic waves incorporating exogenous variables and techniques such as Principal Component Analysis (PCA). The results showed high variability, in the sense that the best-performing models varied across waves. It is concluded that the adaptation of dynamic models is key to improving predictive accuracy in changing epidemiological scenarios, as a consistently predominant set of exogenous variables was not identified according to the Permutation-based ANOVA analysis.

title: An Improved YOLOv8 with Interface Feature Enhancement for Microscopic Defect Detection of Single-Layer Cellulose-Based Composite Materials
authors: Dongjun Tang, Yiyi Cao, Feng Yan, Jingyi Wang, Zihan Wang, Jianbin Xiang, Zujie Dai, Jiayi Qu, Xiaohang Shi, Xiaofeng Wang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Under the global “dual carbon” strategy, cellulosebased composite (CBC) is an ideal green substitute for petroleumbased materials, driving increasing industrial demand. However, processing-induced microdefects in single-layer CBC severely degrade their performance, necessitating efficient and accurate defect detection. Conventional manual inspection is inefficient, subjective, and non-quantitative, whereas existing automated models suffer from a lack of dedicated datasets and poor performance on low-contrast CBC microdefects. To tackle these issues, this study constructs a dedicated dataset with standard YOLO annotations, encompassing typical defects from mainstream preparation processes. We propose an improved YOLOv8-BiCBAM model, which embeds a Dual-Branch Attention Module (DBAM) into the C2f module of YOLOv8 to enhance low-contrast feature extraction, and optimizes the Bidirectional Feature Pyramid Network (BiFPN) for cross-scale feature fusion. Experimental results demonstrate that the model achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$73.4 \% \text{mAP} {@} 0.5,86.1 \%$</tex> precision, 68.7 % recall, and 232 FPS, outperforming mainstream models in accuracy, real-time capability, and robustness. This work fills the gap in CBC-specific defect datasets, provides a reliable technical solution for industrial online inspection, and promotes the high-quality green development of the CBC industry.

title: Machine Learning-Driven Resource Utilization Prediction for Scalable Cloud Infrastructure Management
authors: Hari Prasad Badiginchala
abstract: Contemporary serverless architectures are fine-grained and dynamic, thereby rendering it difficult to optimize resource usage. The multi-resource needs and the increasing variability of workloads make the predictive solutions accurate and scalable. Lack of flexibility in the adaption of the traditional rule-based method to changing workloads is likely to bring about inefficiency. To address this, a detailed machine learning model was developed based on real-world cloud workload traces, and it contained data cleaning, feature engineering, and normalization. The proposed Linear Regression (LR) was experimentally tested on the Microsoft Azure Trace 2017 dataset, compared with the existing ML strategy. The LR model has the highest coefficient of determination (R2=97.32), compared with Cat Boost (66.70), XGBoost (95.00), and Random Forest (0.82). These findings indicate superiority of L R model compared to ensemble-based models. The comparison shows how ensemble-based techniques perform well in predicting accurately, scalably and in real time the utilization of the cloud resources. The conclusion of the paper is that the proposed framework with LR is a strong, feasible solution to enhance efficiency, cost-reduction, and resource allocation intelligency in the modern cloud environments.

title: Exploration of CLIP with Greedy Gradient Ensemble in Visual Question Answering
authors: Yu Pan
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Visual Question Answering (VQA) performance hinges on the effective integration of visual and textual information, where the choice of feature representation and its subsequent adaptation are critical. To investigate the applicability of pre-trained multimodal features within the Greedy Gradient Ensemble (GGE) framework, this study employs CLIP as the visual feature source and conducts experiments on the COCO dataset. We design and compare three CLIP feature adaptation strategies: linear mapping, uniform weight assignment, and a simplified attention mechanism. The value of incorporating a dedicated debiasing strategy is also explored. Experimental results indicate that the linear mapping strategy achieves the highest overall accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(36.62 \%)$</tex> with superior convergence stability. The uniform weight strategy attains 33.94 % accuracy at the lowest computational cost, while the simplified attention mechanism <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(33.75 \%)$</tex> offers no clear performance advantage despite its added complexity. Notably, applying a debiasing strategy markedly improves performance across all methods-for instance, elevating the uniform weight strategy's accuracy to 36.44 %-and confirms its efficacy in mitigating dataset bias. Although this work does not surpass the performance achievable with traditional features like UpDn, it clarifies effective pathways for adapting CLIP within the GGE framework and demonstrates the significant potential of joint debiasing. The findings establish a practical benchmark for the co-design of feature adaptation and bias mitigation, offering valuable insights for developing lightweight and robust VQA models.

title: Research on Vulnerability Mining Technology of a Private Instant Messaging Protocol Based on Reverse Engineering and State Awareness
authors: Yu Shi, Chengyu Du, Man Chen, Qingbing Ji
publish: 2025 2nd World Conference on Computer and Information Security (WCCIS)
abstract: The security of instant messaging protocols directly impacts user privacy and security, and the privatization of protocols renders traditional vulnerability analysis methods ineffective in comprehensively assessing the security of instant messaging protocols. This paper proposes a vulnerability mining method for proprietary instant messaging protocols based on reverse engineering and state awareness. Starting from the reverse engineering of instant messaging software entities, the method combines traffic data to construct a protocol state machine model for instant messaging. Through active state machine learning algorithms, it gradually infers the protocol's temporal logic and state transition rules, thereby obtaining the protocol state machine model for instant messaging. By performing a deep traversal of the protocol state machine, anomalous injection tests are constructed, response data and response states are analyzed and compared, and vulnerabilities in private instant messaging protocols are identified.

title: Multi-Scale Temporal-Attention EEGNet for Automated Epileptic Seizure Detection
authors: Peiyun He
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Traditional manual interpretation of epileptic electroencephalogram (EEG) signals is time-consuming and highly subjective, while existing automated algorithms still suffer from limited feature representation and inadequate identification of critical time segments. To address these issues, this study proposes a lightweight multi-scale temporal attention network, named MS-TA-EEGNet (Multi-Scale Temporal-Attention EEG Network). The model extracts local features across different rhythms via a three-branch multi-scale convolution module (with kernel sizes of 3, 5, and 7), and adopts a pyramid convolution structure to achieve efficient feature abstraction and downsampling. Meanwhile, a temporal attention mechanism is introduced to adaptively enhance the key time steps associated with epileptic seizures, thereby improving the discriminative ability while maintaining a compact architecture. Experiments were conducted on the Bonn Dataset and UCI Epilepsy Recognition Dataset using 10-fold cross-validation. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves high accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity, while maintaining strong cross-population generalization and low computational cost. This study provides an efficient and reliable solution for the clinical application of automated epileptic seizure detection and intelligent EEG analysis systems.

title: Research on Anomaly Detection Methods in Power Marketing Based on Rule-Driven and Data-Driven Fusion
authors: Caihong Ren, Dadi Wang, Qingbo Peng, Zhenglin Li, Qi Huang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of digitalization and refined operation in power marketing businesses, marketing systems have gradually accumulated large-scale, multi-dimensional business data covering metering, electricity pricing, power consumption, and electricity billing and settlement. The types of anomalies exhibit characteristics of diversity, concealment, and combination. Traditional anomaly detection methods that rely on a single rule set or a single data model have inherent limitations in terms of anomaly coverage, detection accuracy, and business interpretability. To address these issues, a power marketing anomaly detection method that integrates rule-driven and data-driven approaches is proposed. This method takes the existing business rules of the marketing system as its foundation and employs a rule-driven mechanism to rapidly identify explicit business anomalies. At the same time, data-driven models are introduced to complement the detection of rule blind spots and complex anomalies. At the application layer, a fusion decision mechanism is constructed to integrate the results from rule-based detection and model-based detection. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms approaches based on a single rule or a single model in terms of anomaly detection accuracy, recall rate, and the capability to identify complex anomalies. It effectively enhances anomaly monitoring and risk prevention in power marketing systems and shows strong engineering applicability Abstract.

title: Real-Time Optimization of Manufacturing Processes and Dynamic Resource Allocation Based on Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Ningya Yu
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: The current manufacturing industry is accelerating towards intelligence. Industrial edge computing, with its advantages of low latency and high reliability, enables real-time collection and local processing of manufacturing data. Machine vision, relying on its high-precision detection capabilities, has become a core technology for quality inspection in the manufacturing process. Machine learning algorithms, as the core support of data-driven methods, occupy an irreplaceable position in manufacturing process optimization and dynamic resource allocation. However, although existing machine learning and deep learning algorithms have been applied in the manufacturing field, they still have obvious shortcomings when dealing with multi-source time series data in the manufacturing process. For this purpose, this paper proposes the RBMO-Attention-BiLSTM classification algorithm. Firstly, correlation analysis and violin graph analysis are carried out, and then its performance is verified through comparative experiments of multiple machine learning algorithms. The experimental results show that the performance of different algorithms in classification tasks varies significantly. The proposed algorithm demonstrates the best comprehensive performance, with accuracy, recall, precision, and F1 value all reaching 99.2%, and the AUC value reaching 100%. All core indicators are significantly better than those of other comparison algorithms. The proposal of this algorithm provides an efficient solution for processing multi-source time series data in manufacturing, which is of great significance for promoting the intelligent upgrade and quality improvement of the manufacturing industry.

title: Zero-Shot Recommendation via Multi-Dimensional User Profiling: A Side-Channel Approach to Cold-Start Optimization
authors: Ying Ren, Xinbao Liu, Xinlin Zhao, Weilong Li, Zhiyan Wang, Yan Liang, Feifei Wang, Liming Zhang, Qiuye Yu
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: The cold-start problem remains a persistent challenge in recommender systems, particularly for new users who lack historical interaction data. Traditional Collaborative Filtering (CF) methods struggle in this zero-shot scenario, often reverting to non-personalized baselines such as popularity-based recommendations. This paper proposes a novel Zero-Shot Profiling Framework that leverages available side-channel information—specifically geospatial data, demographic attributes, and browsing habits—to generate personalized recommendations immediately upon user entry. By mapping user profile vectors to item attribute vectors through a multi-dimensional similarity function, our approach bypasses the need for initial interaction history. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a significantly higher Hit Rate@K in the initial time steps compared to standard baselines, validating its agility and effectiveness in cold-start environments.

title: Integrative Analysis of Gut Microbiome Profiles in Rheumatoid Arthritis Using Differential Abundance, Beta Diversity, and Sparse Machine Learning
authors: Hatim Palitanawala, Sabira Dabeer, Ramprasad Goturkarnam, Santosh Kumar, Shamseerahammed Thendan Kandiyil
abstract: Rheumatoid arthritis (RA) is a systemic autoimmune disease in which the gut microbiome is thought to contribute to disease initiation and progression. We analyzed a large publicly available 16 S rRNA amplicon dataset from the AsiaPacific Rheumatoid Arthritis Cohort (APRAC), comprising 1,034 RA patients and 1,204 healthy controls (HC). Amplicon sequence variants (ASVs) were aggregated to the genus level and analyzed using differential abundance testing (DESeq2), beta-diversity analysis (Bray-Curtis dissimilarity, PCoA, PERMANOVA), and supervised machine learning models (Random Forest, XGBoost, SVM, and LASSO logistic regression). DESeq2 identified 63 significantly different ASVs (adjusted <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p<0.05$</tex>), with 29 enriched in RA and 34 enriched in HC. PERMANOVA confirmed significant community-level differences between groups (pseudo-F <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=$</tex> 32.18, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{p}=0.0001$</tex>). Machine learning models achieved moderate discrimination, with XGBoost performing best (ROC-AUC = 0.8149). These results show that RA is associated with subtle but distributed gut microbiome alterations that provide moderate predictive value for disease classification.

title: An Ambient Assisted Finite Element Approach for DLR Evaluation of Transmission Lines Based on FERC Order 881
authors: Lava Kumar Vandrangi, Zeeshan Rashid
abstract: The ever increasing demand of electrical energy throughout the world has inevitably caused replacement of generation and distribution components. The replacement of high voltage overhead transmission lines is the most difficult, time taking and costly process due to which sufficient effort is put to avoid their replacement. So their capacity analysis is considered a vital task whenever a system upgrade call is approved and maximum possibilities of running them without replacement are explored. Such capacity analysis involves the thermal modeling of conductor cross section in various ambient conditions of temperature, humidity, irradiance and wind intensity. This paper proposes thermal analysis of an overhead high voltage transmission line using finite element method (FEM) aiming at evaluating the ampacity under different ambient conditions. The effect of all four environmental conditions is applied one by one keeping the others constant to subsequently plot the line resistance and ampacity. As a result, new dynamic line rating (DLR) is calculated for each case and plotted along with the static line rating (SLR) for a fair comparison. It is concluded that the DLR is substantially higher than the SLR which is constant as given on the datasheet and is designed based on the worst environmental conditions.

title: Simulation on Right-Angle Surface Transition for a Four-Track Wall-Climbing Robot
authors: Hongtao Shu, Zhenhua Duan, Shengliang You, Chenghao Chen, Yongwei Zhang, Yuxuan Zhu
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: To address the operational demand of wall-climbing robots for traversing right-angle surface transitions on steel substrates, a split-type four-track structural configuration is proposed to enhance the robot's adaptability and stability when crossing internal and external right-angle planes. Dynamic transition simulations were conducted in ADAMS: a 3D geometric model was established and imported, followed by the precise definition of kinematic constraints, contact force parameters, and drive functions. During continuous traversal across two orthogonal steel surfaces, variations in the robot's displacement, velocity, and drive wheel torque were monitored and analyzed in real time. Simulation results demonstrate reliable wall adhesion, smooth right-angle traversal, and no unstable behaviors like slippage or overturning, validating the structural feasibility and simulation effectiveness. This study provides valuable theoretical guidance and simulation references for the optimization of similar wall-climbing robots.

title: Cell-Based Architecture for Solving Data Integrity in Microservices
authors: Supriya Lal
abstract: Cell-based architecture is gaining traction as a solution to challenges such as fault tolerance, scalability, latency, and organizational alignment. This paper explores how sharing databases between microservices within a cell can reduce data integrity issues associated with distributed transactions and introduces strategies to manage the operational challenges of this approach.

title: From Bits to Trust: AI-Driven Reconfigurable Security
authors: Rye Stahle-Smith, Rasha Karakchi
abstract: Reconfigurable computing platforms such as FieldProgrammable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) are widely deployed in embedded systems and AI acceleration workloads. Their reconfigurability, however, also introduces security risks when hardware behavior is defined by externally supplied configuration bitstreams. Maliciously crafted bitstreams can embed hardware Trojans capable of leaking data, degrading system reliability, or altering computation. This paper presents BLADEI (Bitstream-Level Anomaly Detection for Embedded Inference), an AI-based predictive modeling framework for detecting malicious FPGA configurations directly from binary bitstreams. A 278-dimensional feature vector combining byte-frequency histograms, statistical summaries, and structural indicators is extracted from each bitstream and classified using supervised machine learning models. The proposed approach is evaluated on an expanded dataset of 1,090 bitstreams spanning thirteen design families re-engineered for the Xilinx PYNQ-Z1 platform, augmented through Vivado place and route directives to improve dataset diversity. Experimental results demonstrate that a Random Forest classifier achieves a macro F1-score of 0.91 on held-out test data while remaining feasible for on-device deployment, validating the effectiveness of AI-driven bitstream screening in resource-constrained environments.

title: Evaluating Time Series Databases Across Wide and Narrow Schema Designs for Appliance-Level Residential Energy Monitoring
authors: Simin Shehbaz, Mohammad Mehabadi, Kenneth B. Kent
abstract: Appliance-level residential energy monitoring generates high-frequency, high-dimensional time series data that places significant demands on ingestion throughput, storage efficiency, and query performance. While time series databases (TSDBs) are widely used for such workloads, the impact of schema design—wide versus narrow—remains under-explored in the context of residential energy analytics. This paper presents a systematic benchmark of five open-source TSDBs—TimescaleDB, ClickHouse, QuestDB, InfluxDB v1.8, and Apache IoTDB—evaluating their suitability for appliance-level, per-minute energy data across five increasingly intensive workloads. Extending the TSM-Bench methodology, we assess ingestion latency, storage efficiency, and query performance for representative analytical patterns using point and analytical queries. Our results demonstrate that for these specific high-dimensional workloads, schema design can have a performance impact comparable to the underlying database architecture. Under our experimental configurations, wide schemas consistently outperformed narrow schemas across ingestion and query operations, while ClickHouse uniquely sustained efficient performance at the billion-row scale under both formats. These findings provide evidence-based guidance for TSDB selection and schema design, highlighting that the optimal database choice is highly dependent on the intersection of data dimensionality and intended schema format.

title: Research on the Crowdsourced Security Testing Contest Based on a Screening Theory
authors: Zhongfeng Jin, Ling Liu, Chenhao Wu, Xiyu Lu, Dingyu Yan
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Crowd-sourced security testing is a novel testing mode that attracts testing professionals through a reward-based approach to participate in the discovery of security risks in IT products. However, there are several issues in the practice of crowdsourced testing projects, such as insufficient understanding of testing specialists, the unreasonable configuration of testers and projects. A mathematical condition for contest configuration based on the screening theory is proposed on the basis of the model for crowdsourced security testing from the perspective of economics, aiming at optimizing the configuration of crowdsourced security testing contest. Numerical simulations verify the theoretical condition can enable testers with different skill levels to adaptively choose the corresponding contest. Moreover, based on the optimized contest configuration, this research analyzes the impact of contest parameters on the testers' own expected utility and effort levels. Numerical results indicate that an increase in the number of testers intensifies competition and higher-skilled testers are more willing to exert greater effort to obtain higher expected utility.

title: Over-Smoothing Effect of Graph Convolutional Networks: Spectral and Topological Analysis with Practical Remedies
authors: Fang Sun
abstract: Graph convolutional networks (GCNs) are effective for node classification but their depth is limited by oversmoothing: repeated propagation makes node features within connected components indistinguishable. We present a concise theoretical account of over-smoothing and its practical implications. From spectral and topological perspectives, we formalize smoothness and derive an upper bound that characterizes when exponential over-smoothing occurs as depth grows, in terms of the normalized Laplacian spectrum and network kernel norms. The analysis explains why several remedies-graph sparsification, depth-leveraging across hops, and architectures with initial/residual connections-alleviate over-smoothing. Empirically, on Cora, accuracy degrades beyond a small number of layers even without overfitting or vanishing gradients, consistent with the theory. Together, these results provide simple conditions for anticipating over-smoothing and a unified lens for selecting effective mitigation strategies.

title: Assistant Diagnosis of Glaucoma Optimizing Subtle Lesion Detection and Multi-Scale Feature Extraction
authors: Hongkai Zhang, Chao Ma, Chao Li
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Glaucoma is a common chronic eye disease that leads to irreversible vision loss. Existing glaucoma screening methods typically rely on precise segmentation of key structures (e.g., optic disc and optic cup), but they often overlook other important visual features. Moreover, the diverse and multiscale nature of glaucomatous lesions increases the difficulty of diagnosis. To address these challenges, this paper proposes the Atrous Global Coordinate Attention Network (AGCA-Net), a novel multi-scale fusion framework for the diagnosis of glaucoma on fundus images. Firstly, precise optic disc segmentation masks are extracted to highlight pathological regions while suppressing background interference. A local branch then extracts fine-grained structural features of disc lesions, while a global branch captures holistic fundus characteristics. The fused features enable accurate pathological analysis. Specifically, we first introduce atrous convolution to expand the receptive field, preserve image details, and capture pathological changes at multiple scales, thereby enhancing sensitivity to glaucomatous lesions. Additionally, we incorporate the Coordinate Attention (CA) mechanism into the model to improve the perception of the optic disc and other pathological regions, thereby enhancing the recognition of subtle features. Furthermore, we design an auxiliary feature fusion module to integrate local and global features, compensating for critical information that may be lost during structural segmentation, reducing redundant features, and improving model robustness. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance on multiple public glaucoma datasets, including RIM-ONE-DL, REFUGE, and ORIGA. Notably, on the RIM-ONE-DL dataset, the model improves accuracy by 8.33% and AUC by 8.19%, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art methods.

title: A Method for Modeling Photovoltaic Characteristics Under Artificial Partial Shading
authors: Aleck W. Leedy, Jacob C Birdsong
abstract: A curve-fitting method for approximating the power-voltage characteristics of a photovoltaic module under artificial shading is presented. The measured P-V curves are approximated using a fourth-order polynomial model. The coefficients of this model are strongly dependent on the percentage of cells shaded. By interpolating the polynomial coefficients, this method enables the approximation of PV behavior at intermediate levels of shading. A leave-one-out validation method was utilized, resulting in a high coefficient of determination. The method presented has the potential to be used in maximum power point tracking (MPPT) algorithms.

title: Can Domain Transfer Counter Adversarial Attacks in Traffic Sign Classification Systems?
authors: Mulham Fawakherji, Ahmad Patooghy
abstract: Deep learning-based traffic sign classification systems achieve high accuracy under clean conditions but remain highly vulnerable to adversarial perturbations, posing serious safety risks for advanced driver assistance systems and autonomous vehicles. This paper introduces a CycleGAN-based adversarial reversal framework that models adversarial perturbations as a domain shift and restores attacked images to their clean semantic representation prior to the classification task, without modifying the underlying classifier. Applying the proposed CycleGAN-based restoration significantly enhances robustness, recovering accuracy by up to 72 %, achieving approximately 90 % accuracy for CNN models and 97.23 % for ViT-16 under attack conditions. These results demonstrate that generative adversarial reversal provides an effective and model-agnostic defense strategy for safety-critical perception systems.

title: Secure by Design: Quantifying Architectural Resilience and the Agents Rule of two
authors: Qianlong Lan, Anuj Kaul, Shaun Jones, Stephanie Westrum
abstract: The transition of Large Language Models (LLMs) into autonomous agents equipped with tools necessitates a security paradigm shift. Current defenses typically rely on prompt engineering, which has proven insufficient to defend against Indirect Prompt Injection (IPI). This paper presents a quantitative evaluation of architectural defense patterns specifically Plan-then-Execute (P-t-E) and Context-Minimization (Context-Min), which are integrated under the strategic guidance of the Agents Rule of Two (R2). Advanced attack vectors on four distinct agent architectures, including IPI parameter hijacking and Attractive Metadata Attacks (AMA) were conducted.
